query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: c0066b4a).

## Issue to Resolve

Remove default for allowed wildcard imports in `ktlint_official` codestyle
>Expected Behavior
> When java.util.* is included as a wildcard import, it should trigger the warning for no-wildcard-imports.
> ...
> Given that, I think it makes sense that ktlint should stop including those two paths as default wildcard exceptions.

_Originally posted by @greg-patterson in https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1792#issuecomment-1407098815_

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

The feature to respect setting ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand  was implemented not too long ago in ktlint 0.45.x via https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1272. The reason for adding the default values is that they seems to be hard-coded in IntelliJ IDEA as well (https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82).

If the property is not set explicitly, IntelliJ IDEA allows imports java.util.* and kotlinx.android.synthetic.*. If ktlint would not do the same, this would result in a conclict between ktlint and IntelliJ IDEA. Lots of users of ktlint dislike that.

In the new codestyle `ktlint_official` the default value for allowed wildcard imports shoulbe be cleared.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `.editorconfig`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `docs/rules/standard.md`
- `ktlint-cli/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/cli/internal/KtlintCommandLine.kt`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/RuleExecutionContext.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.importordering.PatternEntry
  9→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
 12→
 13→public class NoWildcardImportsRule :
 14→    StandardRule(
 15→        id = "no-wildcard-imports",
 16→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 17→            IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND,
 18→        ),
 19→    ) {
 20→    private lateinit var allowedWildcardImports: List<PatternEntry>
 21→
 22→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 23→        allowedWildcardImports = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND]
 24→    }
 25→
 26→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 27→        node: ASTNode,
 28→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 29→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 30→    ) {
 31→        if (node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
 32→            val importDirective = node.psi as KtImportDirective
 33→            val path = importDirective.importPath ?: return
 34→            if (!path.isAllUnder) return
 35→            if (allowedWildcardImports.none { it.matches(path) }) {
 36→                emit(node.startOffset, "Wildcard import", false)
 37→            }
 38→        }
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    public companion object {
 42→        private const val WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES = "*"
 43→        private const val WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES = "**"
 44→
 45→        private fun parseAllowedWildcardImports(allowedWildcardImports: String): List<PatternEntry> {
 46→            val importsList = allowedWildcardImports.split(",").onEach { it.trim() }
 47→
 48→            return importsList.map { import ->
 49→                if (import.endsWith(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES)) { // java.**
 50→                    PatternEntry(
 51→                        packageName = import.removeSuffix(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES).plus(WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES),
 52→                        withSubpackages = true,
 53→                        hasAlias = false,
 54→                    )
 55→                } else {
 56→                    PatternEntry(
 57→                        packageName = import,
 58→                        withSubpackages = false,
 59→                        hasAlias = false,
 60→                    )
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        private val PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
 66→            { _, value ->
 67→                when {
 68→                    else -> try {
 69→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
 70→                            value,
 71→                            value?.let(Companion::parseAllowedWildcardImports) ?: emptyList(),
 72→                        )
 73→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 74→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
 75→                            value,
 76→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
 77→                        )
 78→                    }
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→
 82→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
 83→            EditorConfigProperty(
 84→                type = PropertyType(
 85→                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
 86→                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
 87→                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
 88→                ),
 89→                /**
 90→                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
 91→                 * it's subpackages.
 92→                 *
 93→                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
 94→                 */
 95→                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
 96→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
 97→            )
 98→    }
 99→}
100→
101→public val NO_WILDCARD_IMPORTS_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoWildcardImportsRule().ruleId
102→

229→

232→        description = ["Defines the minimum log level (trace, debug, info, warn, error) or none to suppress all logging"],

239→    private val errorNumber = AtomicInteger()

242→        get() = Level.DEBUG.isGreaterOrEqual(minLogLevel)
243→        private set
244→
245→    private fun disabledRulesEditorConfigOverrides() =
246→        disabledRules
247→            .split(",")
248→            .filter { it.isNotBlank() }
249→            .map {
250→                // For backwards compatibility, prefix the rule id with the standard rule set id when missing
251→                RuleId.prefixWithStandardRuleSetIdWhenMissing(it)
252→            }.map { RuleId(it).createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.disabled }
253→            .toTypedArray()
254→
255→    fun run() {
256→        val editorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride
257→            .EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE
258→            .applyIf(experimental) {
259→                logger.debug { "Add editor config override to allow the experimental rule set" }
260→                plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)
261→            }.applyIf(disabledRules.isNotBlank()) {
262→                logger.debug { "Add editor config override to disable rules: '$disabledRules'" }
263→                plus(*disabledRulesEditorConfigOverrides())
264→            }.applyIf(android) {
265→                logger.debug { "Add editor config override to set code style to 'android'" }
266→                plus(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY to CodeStyleValue.android)
267→            }.applyIf(stdin) {
268→                logger.debug {
269→                    "Add editor config override to disable 'filename' rule which can not be used in combination with reading from <stdin>"
270→                }
271→                plus(FILENAME_RULE_ID.createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.disabled)
272→            }
273→
274→        if (android) {
275→            logger.error {
276→                "Option '--android' / '-a' is deprecated and replaced with option '--code-style=android_studio'. Setting '.editorconfig' " +
277→                    "property 'ktlint_code_style=android_studio' might be a better idea for a project that is always to formatted with " +
278→                    "this code style."
279→            }
280→        }
281→        assertStdinAndPatternsFromStdinOptionsMutuallyExclusive()
282→        patterns = patterns.replaceWithPatternsFromStdinOrDefaultPatternsWhenEmpty()
283→
284→        val start = System.currentTimeMillis()
285→
286→        val ruleProviders = ruleProviders()
287→        val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
288→            ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
289→            editorConfigDefaults = editorConfigDefaults(ruleProviders),
290→            editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride,
291→            isInvokedFromCli = true,
292→        )
293→
294→        val baseline = if (stdin || baselinePath.isBlank()) {
295→            Baseline(status = Baseline.Status.DISABLED)
296→        } else {
297→            loadBaseline(baselinePath)
298→        }
299→
300→        val aggregatedReporter = ReporterAggregator(
301→            baseline,
302→            reporterConfigurations,
303→            color,
304→            colorName,
305→            stdin,
306→            format,
307→            relative,
308→        ).aggregatedReporter()
309→
310→        aggregatedReporter.beforeAll()
311→        if (stdin) {
312→            lintStdin(
313→                ktLintRuleEngine,
314→                aggregatedReporter,
315→            )
316→        } else {
317→            lintFiles(
318→                ktLintRuleEngine,
319→                baseline.lintErrorsPerFile,
320→                aggregatedReporter,
321→            )
322→        }
323→        aggregatedReporter.afterAll()
324→
325→        logger.debug {
326→            "Finished processing in ${System.currentTimeMillis() - start}ms / $fileNumber file(s) scanned / $errorNumber error(s) found"
327→        }
328→        if (fileNumber.get() == 0) {
329→            // Do not return an error as this would implicate that in a multi-module project, each module has to contain
330→            // at least one kotlin file.
331→            logger.warn { "No files matched $patterns" }
332→        }
333→        if (tripped.get()) {
334→            exitKtLintProcess(1)
335→        } else {
336→            exitKtLintProcess(0)
337→        }
338→    }
339→
340→    private fun editorConfigDefaults(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigDefaults {
341→        val fullyExpandedEditorConfigPath =
342→            editorConfigPath
343→                ?.expandTildeToFullPath()
344→                ?.let { path -> Paths.get(path) }
345→        return EditorConfigDefaults.load(fullyExpandedEditorConfigPath, ruleProviders.propertyTypes())
346→    }
347→
348→    private fun List<String>.replaceWithPatternsFromStdinOrDefaultPatternsWhenEmpty(): List<String> {
349→        val localStdinDelimiter: String? = stdinDelimiter
350→        return when {
351→            localStdinDelimiter != null -> {
352→                val stdinPatterns: Set<String> = readPatternsFromStdin(localStdinDelimiter.ifEmpty { "\u0000" })
353→                if (isNotEmpty() && stdinPatterns.isNotEmpty()) {
354→                    logger.warn {
355→                        "Patterns specified at command line ($this) and patterns from 'stdin' due to flag '--patterns-from-stdin' " +
356→                            "($stdinPatterns) are merged"
357→                    }
358→                }
359→                // Note: it is okay in case both the original patterns and the patterns from stdin are empty
360→                this.plus(stdinPatterns)
361→            }
362→            this.isEmpty() -> {
363→                logger.info { "Enable default patterns $DEFAULT_PATTERNS" }
364→                DEFAULT_PATTERNS
365→            }
366→            else -> {
367→                // Keep original patterns
368→                this
369→            }
370→        }
371→    }
372→
373→    // Do not convert to "val" as the function depends on PicoCli options which are not fully instantiated until the "run" method is started
374→    internal fun ruleProviders(): Set<RuleProvider> = loadRuleProviders(rulesetJarPaths.toFilesURIList())
375→
376→    // Do not convert to "val" as the function depends on PicoCli options which are not fully instantiated until the "run" method is started
377→    internal fun configureLogger() {
378→        logger = KotlinLogging
379→            .logger {}
380→            .setDefaultLoggerModifier { logger ->
381→                (logger.underlyingLogger as Logger).level = minLogLevel
382→            }
383→            .initKtLintKLogger()
384→    }
385→
386→    private fun assertStdinAndPatternsFromStdinOptionsMutuallyExclusive() {
387→        if (stdin && stdinDelimiter != null) {
388→            throw ParameterException(
389→                commandSpec.commandLine(),
390→                "Options --stdin and --patterns-from-stdin are mutually exclusive",
391→            )
392→        }
393→    }
394→
395→    private fun lintFiles(
396→        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
397→        lintErrorsPerFile: Map<String, List<KtlintCliError>>,
398→        reporter: ReporterV2,
399→    ) {
400→        FileSystems
401→            .getDefault()
402→            .fileSequence(patterns)
403→            .map { it.toFile() }
404→            .takeWhile { errorNumber.get() < limit }
405→            .map { file ->
406→                val fileName = file.toPath().absolutePathString()
407→                Callable {
408→                    fileName to process(
409→                        ktLintRuleEngine = ktLintRuleEngine,
410→                        code = Code.fromFile(file),
411→                        baselineLintErrors = lintErrorsPerFile.getOrDefault(fileName, emptyList()),
412→                    )
413→                }
414→            }.parallel({ (fileName, errList) -> report(Paths.get(fileName).relativeRoute, errList, reporter) })
415→    }
416→
417→    private fun lintStdin(
418→        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
419→        reporter: ReporterV2,
420→    ) {
421→        report(
422→            KtLintRuleEngine.STDIN_FILE,
423→            process(
424→                ktLintRuleEngine = ktLintRuleEngine,
425→                code = Code.fromStdin(),
426→                baselineLintErrors = emptyList(),
427→            ),
428→            reporter,
429→        )
430→    }
431→
432→    private fun report(
433→        fileName: String,
434→        ktlintCliErrors: List<KtlintCliError>,
435→        reporter: ReporterV2,
436→    ) {
437→        fileNumber.incrementAndGet()
438→        val errListLimit = minOf(ktlintCliErrors.size, maxOf(limit - errorNumber.get(), 0))
439→        errorNumber.addAndGet(errListLimit)
440→
441→        val relativeRoute = Paths.get(fileName).relativeRoute
442→        reporter.before(relativeRoute)
443→        ktlintCliErrors
444→            .take(errListLimit)
445→            .forEach { reporter.onLintError(relativeRoute, it) }
446→        reporter.after(relativeRoute)
447→    }
448→
449→    private fun process(
450→        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
451→        code: Code,
452→        baselineLintErrors: List<KtlintCliError>,
453→    ): List<KtlintCliError> {
454→        if (code.fileName != null) {
455→            logger.trace { "Checking ${File(code.fileName!!).location(relative)}" }
456→        }
457→        val result = ArrayList<KtlintCliError>()
458→        if (format) {
459→            val formattedFileContent = try {
460→                ktLintRuleEngine.format(code) { lintError, corrected ->
461→                    val ktlintCliError = KtlintCliError(
462→                        line = lintError.line,
463→                        col = lintError.col,
464→                        ruleId = lintError.ruleId,
465→                        detail = lintError
466→                            .detail
467→                            .applyIf(corrected) { "$this (cannot be auto-corrected)" },
468→                        status = if (corrected) {
469→                            FORMAT_IS_AUTOCORRECTED
470→                        } else {
471→                            LINT_CAN_NOT_BE_AUTOCORRECTED
472→                        },
473→                    )
474→                    if (baselineLintErrors.doesNotContain(ktlintCliError)) {
475→                        result.add(ktlintCliError)
476→                        if (!corrected) {
477→                            tripped.set(true)
478→                        }
479→                    }
480→                }
481→            } catch (e: Exception) {
482→                result.add(e.toKtlintCliError(code))
483→                tripped.set(true)
484→                code.content // making sure `cat file | ktlint --stdin > file` is (relatively) safe
485→            }
486→            when {
487→                code.isStdIn -> print(formattedFileContent)
488→                code.content != formattedFileContent ->
489→                    code
490→                        .filePath
491→                        ?.toFile()
492→                        ?.writeText(formattedFileContent, charset("UTF-8"))
493→            }
494→        } else {
495→            try {
496→                ktLintRuleEngine.lint(code) { lintError ->
497→                    val ktlintCliError = KtlintCliError(
498→                        line = lintError.line,
499→                        col = lintError.col,
500→                        ruleId = lintError.ruleId,
501→                        detail = lintError.detail,
502→                        status = if (lintError.canBeAutoCorrected) {
503→                            LINT_CAN_BE_AUTOCORRECTED
504→                        } else {
505→                            LINT_CAN_NOT_BE_AUTOCORRECTED
506→                        },
507→                    )
508→                    if (baselineLintErrors.doesNotContain(ktlintCliError)) {
509→                        result.add(ktlintCliError)
510→                        tripped.set(true)
511→                    }
512→                }
513→            } catch (e: Exception) {
514→                result.add(e.toKtlintCliError(code))
515→                tripped.set(true)
516→            }
517→        }
518→        return result
519→    }
520→
521→    private fun Exception.toKtlintCliError(code: Code): KtlintCliError =
522→        this.let { e ->
523→            when (e) {
524→                is KtLintParseException ->
525→                    KtlintCliError(
526→                        line = e.line,
527→                        col = e.col,
528→                        ruleId = "",
529→                        detail = "Not a valid Kotlin file (${e.message?.lowercase(Locale.getDefault())})",
530→                        status = KOTLIN_PARSE_EXCEPTION,
531→                    )
532→                is KtLintRuleException -> {
533→                    val codeSource = if (code.isStdIn) {
534→                        "code"
535→                    } else {
536→                        "file '${code.fileName}'"
537→                    }
538→                    logger.debug("Internal Error (${e.ruleId}) in $codeSource at position '${e.line}:${e.col}", e)
539→                    KtlintCliError(
540→                        line = e.line,
541→                        col = e.col,
542→                        ruleId = "",
543→                        detail = "Internal Error (rule '${e.ruleId}') in $codeSource at position '${e.line}:${e.col}. " +
544→                            "Please create a ticket at https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues " +
545→                            "and provide the source code that triggered an error.\n" +
546→                            e.stackTraceToString(),
547→                        status = KTLINT_RULE_ENGINE_EXCEPTION,
548→                    )
549→                }
550→                else -> throw e
551→            }
552→        }
553→
554→    private fun readPatternsFromStdin(delimiter: String): Set<String> {
555→        require(delimiter.isNotEmpty())
556→
557→        return String(System.`in`.readBytes())
558→            .split(delimiter)
559→            .let { patterns: List<String> ->
560→                patterns.filterTo(LinkedHashSet(patterns.size), String::isNotEmpty)
561→            }
562→    }
563→
564→    /**
565→     * Executes "Callable"s in parallel (lazily).
566→     * The results are gathered one-by-one (by `cb(<callable result>)`) in the order of corresponding "Callable"s
567→     * in the "Sequence" (think `seq.toList().map { executorService.submit(it) }.forEach { cb(it.get()) }` but without
568→     * buffering an entire sequence).
569→     *
570→     * Once kotlinx-coroutines are out of "experimental" stage everything below can be replaced with
571→     * ```
572→     * suspend fun <T> Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel(...) {
573→     *     val ctx = newFixedThreadPoolContext(numberOfThreads, "Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel")
574→     *     ctx.use {
575→     *         val channel = produce(ctx, numberOfThreads) {
576→     *             for (task in this@parallel) {
577→     *                 send(async(ctx) { task.call() })
578→     *             }
579→     *         }
580→     *         for (res in channel) {
581→     *             cb(res.await())
582→     *         }
583→     *     }
584→     * }
585→     * ```
586→     */
587→    private fun <T> Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel(
588→        cb: (T) -> Unit,
589→        numberOfThreads: Int = Runtime.getRuntime().availableProcessors(),
590→    ) {
591→        val pill = object : Future<T> {
592→            override fun isDone(): Boolean {
593→                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
594→            }
595→
596→            override fun get(
597→                timeout: Long,
598→                unit: TimeUnit,
599→            ): T {
600→                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
601→            }
602→
603→            override fun get(): T {
604→                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
605→            }
606→
607→            override fun cancel(mayInterruptIfRunning: Boolean): Boolean {
608→                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
609→            }
610→
611→            override fun isCancelled(): Boolean {
612→                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
613→            }
614→        }
615→        val q = ArrayBlockingQueue<Future<T>>(numberOfThreads)
616→        val producer = thread(start = true) {
617→            val executorService = Executors.newCachedThreadPool()
618→            try {
619→                for (task in this) {
620→                    q.put(executorService.submit(task))
621→                }
622→                q.put(pill)
623→            } catch (e: InterruptedException) {
624→                // we've been asked to stop consuming sequence
625→            } finally {
626→                executorService.shutdown()
627→            }
628→        }
629→        try {
630→            while (true) {
631→                val result = q.take()
632→                if (result != pill) cb(result.get()) else break
633→            }
634→        } finally {
635→            producer.interrupt() // in case q.take()/result.get() throws
636→            producer.join()
637→        }
638→    }
639→}
640→
641→private class CodeStyleValueConverter : CommandLine.ITypeConverter<CodeStyleValue> {
642→    @Throws(Exception::class)
643→    override fun convert(value: String?): CodeStyleValue =
644→        when (value?.lowercase()?.replace("-", "_")) {
645→            null -> CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
646→            "ktlint_official" -> CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official
647→            "android_studio" -> CodeStyleValue.android_studio
648→            "intellij_idea" -> CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea
649→            else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid code style value")
650→        }
651→}
652→
653→private class LogLevelConverter : CommandLine.ITypeConverter<Level> {
654→    @Throws(Exception::class)
655→    override fun convert(value: String?): Level =
656→        when (value?.uppercase()) {
657→            "TRACE" -> Level.TRACE
658→            "DEBUG" -> Level.DEBUG
659→            "INFO" -> Level.INFO
660→            "WARN" -> Level.WARN
661→            "ERROR" -> Level.ERROR
662→            "NONE" -> Level.OFF
663→            else -> throw IllegalStateException("Invalid log level '$value'")
664→        }
665→}
666→
667→// Do not convert to "val" as the function depends on PicoCli options which are not fully instantiated until the "run" method is started
668→internal fun List<String>.toFilesURIList() =
669→    map {
670→        val jarFile = File(it.expandTildeToFullPath())
671→        if (!jarFile.exists()) {
672→            logger.error { "File '$it' does not exist" }
673→            exitKtLintProcess(1)
674→        }
675→        jarFile.toURI().toURL()
676→    }
677→
678→/**
679→ * Gets the relative route of the path. Also adjusts the slashes for uniformity between file systems.
680→ */
681→internal val Path.relativeRoute: String
682→    get() {
683→        val rootPath = Paths.get("").toAbsolutePath()
684→        return this
685→            .relativeToOrSelf(rootPath)
686→            .pathString
687→            .replace(File.separatorChar, '/')
688→    }
689→
690→/**
691→ * Wrapper around exitProcess which ensure that a proper log line is written which can be used in unit tests for
692→ * validating the result of the test.
693→ */
694→internal fun exitKtLintProcess(status: Int): Nothing {
695→    logger.debug { "Exit ktlint with exit code: $status" }
696→    exitProcess(status)
697→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintParseException
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine.Companion.UTF8_BOM
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleException
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.RuleExecutionRuleFilter
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.RunAfterRuleFilter
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.ruleRunners
 14→import mu.KotlinLogging
 15→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 16→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.FileASTNode
 17→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiElement
 18→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiErrorElement
 19→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.idea.KotlinLanguage
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtFile
 21→
 22→private val KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER = KotlinPsiFileFactoryProvider()
 23→
 24→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 25→
 26→internal class RuleExecutionContext private constructor(
 27→    val code: Code,
 28→    val rootNode: FileASTNode,
 29→    val ruleRunners: Set<RuleRunner>,
 30→    val editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 31→    val positionInTextLocator: (offset: Int) -> LineAndColumn,
 32→) {
 33→    private lateinit var suppressionLocator: SuppressionLocator
 34→
 35→    init {
 36→        rebuildSuppressionLocator()
 37→    }
 38→
 39→    fun rebuildSuppressionLocator() {
 40→        suppressionLocator = SuppressionLocatorBuilder.buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(rootNode)
 41→    }
 42→
 43→    fun executeRule(
 44→        rule: Rule,
 45→        fqRuleId: String,
 46→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 47→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 48→    ) {
 49→        try {
 50→            rule.startTraversalOfAST()
 51→            rule.beforeFirstNode(
 52→                // The rule get access to an EditConfig which is filtered by the properties which are actually registered as being used by
 53→                // the rule. In this way it can be forced that the rule actually registers the properties that it uses and the field becomes
 54→                // reliable to be used by for example the ".editorconfig" file generator.
 55→                editorConfig.filterBy(rule.usesEditorConfigProperties),
 56→            )
 57→            this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(rootNode, rule, fqRuleId, autoCorrect, emit)
 58→            rule.afterLastNode()
 59→        } catch (e: RuleExecutionException) {
 60→            throw KtLintRuleException(
 61→                e.line,
 62→                e.col,
 63→                e.rule.ruleId.value,
 64→                """
 65→                Rule '${e.rule.ruleId.value}' throws exception in file '${code.fileName}' at position (${e.line}:${e.col})
 66→                   Rule maintainer: ${e.rule.about.maintainer}
 67→                   Issue tracker  : ${e.rule.about.issueTrackerUrl}
 68→                   Repository     : ${e.rule.about.repositoryUrl}
 69→                """.trimIndent(),
 70→                e.cause,
 71→            )
 72→        }
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    private fun executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 76→        node: ASTNode,
 77→        rule: Rule,
 78→        fqRuleId: String,
 79→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 80→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81→    ) {
 82→        /**
 83→         * The [suppressionLocator] can be changed during each visit of node when running [KtLintRuleEngine.format]. So a new handler is to
 84→         * be built before visiting the nodes.
 85→         */
 86→        val suppressHandler = SuppressHandler(suppressionLocator, autoCorrect, emit)
 87→        if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 88→            try {
 89→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 90→                    rule.beforeVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→                }
 92→                if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 93→                    node
 94→                        .getChildren(null)
 95→                        .forEach { childNode ->
 96→                            suppressHandler.handle(childNode, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 97→                                this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 98→                                    childNode,
 99→                                    rule,
100→                                    fqRuleId,
101→                                    autoCorrect,
102→                                    emit,
103→                                )
104→                            }
105→                        }
106→                }
107→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
108→                    rule.afterVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
109→                }
110→            } catch (e: Exception) {
111→                if (autoCorrect) {
112→                    // line/col cannot be reliably mapped as exception might originate from a node not present in the
113→                    // original AST
114→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
115→                        rule,
116→                        0,
117→                        0,
118→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
119→                        e.cause ?: e,
120→                    )
121→                } else {
122→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(node.startOffset)
123→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
124→                        rule,
125→                        line,
126→                        col,
127→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
128→                        e.cause ?: e,
129→                    )
130→                }
131→            }
132→        }
133→    }
134→
135→    companion object {
136→        internal fun createRuleExecutionContext(
137→            ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
138→            code: Code,
139→        ): RuleExecutionContext {
140→            val psiFileFactory = KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER.getKotlinPsiFileFactory(ktLintRuleEngine.isInvokedFromCli)
141→            val normalizedText = normalizeText(code.content)
142→            val positionInTextLocator = buildPositionInTextLocator(normalizedText)
143→
144→            val psiFileName =
145→                code.fileName
146→                    ?: if (code.script) {
147→                        "File.kts"
148→                    } else {
149→                        "File.kt"
150→                    }
151→            val psiFile = psiFileFactory.createFileFromText(
152→                psiFileName,
153→                KotlinLanguage.INSTANCE,
154→                normalizedText,
155→            ) as KtFile
156→
157→            psiFile
158→                .findErrorElement()
159→                ?.let { errorElement ->
160→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(errorElement.textOffset)
161→                    throw KtLintParseException(line, col, errorElement.errorDescription)
162→                }
163→
164→            val rootNode = psiFile.node
165→
166→            val editorConfig =
167→                ktLintRuleEngine
168→                    .editorConfigLoader
169→                    .load(code.filePath)
170→                    .also {
171→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
172→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("disabled_rules", "0.49")
173→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
174→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("ktlint_disabled_rules", "0.49")
175→                    }
176→
177→            val ruleRunners =
178→                ktLintRuleEngine.ruleRunners(
179→                    RuleExecutionRuleFilter(editorConfig),
180→                    RunAfterRuleFilter(),
181→                )
182→
183→            return RuleExecutionContext(
184→                code,
185→                rootNode,
186→                ruleRunners,
187→                editorConfig,
188→                positionInTextLocator,
189→            )
190→        }
191→
192→        private fun normalizeText(text: String): String {
193→            return text
194→                .replace("\r\n", "\n")
195→                .replace("\r", "\n")
196→                .replaceFirst(UTF8_BOM, "")
197→        }
198→
199→        private fun PsiElement.findErrorElement(): PsiErrorElement? {
200→            if (this is PsiErrorElement) {
201→                return this
202→            }
203→            this.children.forEach { child ->
204→                val errorElement = child.findErrorElement()
205→                if (errorElement != null) {
206→                    return errorElement
207→                }
208→            }
209→            return null
210→        }
211→    }
212→}
213→
214→private fun EditorConfig.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete(
215→    propertyName: String,
216→    ktlintVersion: String,
217→) {
218→    if (this.contains(propertyName)) {
219→        LOGGER.warn {
220→            "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' is obsolete and is not used by KtLint starting from version $ktlintVersion. Remove " +
221→                "the property from all '.editorconfig' files."
222→        }
223→    }
224→}
225→
226→private class RuleExecutionException(
227→    val rule: Rule,
228→    val line: Int,
229→    val col: Int,
230→    override val cause: Throwable,
231→) : Throwable(cause)

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2→
 3→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 4→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.EnumValueParser
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * Code style to be used while linting and formatting. Note that the [EnumValueParser] requires values to be lowercase.
 8→ */
 9→@Suppress("EnumEntryName")
10→public enum class CodeStyleValue {
11→    @Deprecated(
12→        message = "Marked for removal in KtLint 0.50. Value is renamed to 'android_studio'.",
13→        replaceWith = ReplaceWith("android_studio"),
14→    )
15→    android,
16→
17→    /**
18→     * Code formatting based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This
19→     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in Android Studio.
20→     */
21→    android_studio,
22→
23→    /**
24→     * Code formatting based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). This
25→     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in IntelliJ IDEA.
26→     */
27→    intellij_idea,
28→
29→    /**
30→     * Code formatting based on the best of both the Kotlin Coding conventions
31→     * (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide
32→     * (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics
33→     * which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats
34→     * code in a way which is not compatible with the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. When
35→     * using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) automatic code formatting in the editor. Mean reason
36→     * for deviating from the code formatting provided by the editor is that those contain bugs which after some years
37→     * are still not fixed.
38→     * In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
39→     */
40→    ktlint_official,
41→
42→    @Deprecated(
43→        message = "Marked for removal in KtLint 0.50. Value is renamed to 'intellij_idea'.",
44→        replaceWith = ReplaceWith("intellij_idea"),
45→    )
46→    official,
47→}
48→
49→public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE: PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType<CodeStyleValue> =
50→    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
51→        "ktlint_code_style",
52→        "The code style ('intellij_idea', 'android_studio' or 'ktlint_official') to be applied. Currently, the " +
53→            "value is defaulted to 'intellij_idea' when not set. However, in the future the default code style " +
54→            "will be changed to 'ktlint_official'.",
55→        SafeEnumValueParser(CodeStyleValue::class.java),
56→        CodeStyleValue.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
57→    )
58→
59→public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<CodeStyleValue> =
60→    EditorConfigProperty(
61→        type = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE,
62→        /**
63→         * Once the [CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official] is matured, it will become the default code style of ktlint. Until
64→         * then the [CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea] is used to remain backwards compatible.
65→         */
66→        defaultValue = CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea,
67→        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.android_studio,
68→        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea,
69→        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official,
70→    )
71→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2→
 3→import org.ec4j.core.model.Property
 4→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * Definition of '.editorconfig' property enriched with fields required by the [com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine].
 8→ *
 9→ * Properties can be:
10→ * - universal `.editorconfig` properties defined
11→ *   [here](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/wiki/EditorConfig-Properties#current-universal-properties)
12→ * - universal IntelliJ IDEA properties defined
13→ *   [here](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-community/blob/master/platform/lang-api/src/com/intellij/psi/codeStyle/CommonCodeStyleSettings.java)
14→ * - Kotlin specific properties defined
15→ *   [here](https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/master/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java)
16→ */
17→public data class EditorConfigProperty<T>(
18→    /**
19→     * Type of property. Could be one of default ones (see [PropertyType.STANDARD_TYPES]) or custom one.
20→     */
21→    public val type: PropertyType<T>,
22→
23→    /**
24→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties. This default applies to all code styles
25→     * unless the code style specific default value is set.
26→     */
27→    public val defaultValue: T,
28→
29→    /**
30→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'ktlint_official'. When not
31→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
32→     */
33→    public val ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
34→
35→    /**
36→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'intellij_idea'. When not
37→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
38→     */
39→    public val intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
40→
41→    /**
42→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'android_studio'. When not
43→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
44→     */
45→    public val androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
46→
47→    /**
48→     * If set, it maps the actual value set for the property, to another valid value for that property. See example
49→     * below where
50→     * ```kotlin
51→     * propertyMapper = { property, isAndroidCodeStyle ->
52→     *     when {
53→     *         property == null ->
54→     *             // property is not defined in ".editorconfig" file
55→     *         property.isUnset ->
56→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with special value "unset"
57→     *         property.sourceValue == "some-string-value" ->
58→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with a value that needs to be remapped to another
59→     *             // valid value. For example the "max_line_length" property accepts value "off" but is remapped to
60→     *             // "-1" in ktlint.
61→     *        else ->
62→     *             property.getValueAs() // or null
63→     *     }
64→     * }
65→     * ```
66→     * In case the lambda returns a null value then, the code style specific default value or the generic [defaultValue]
67→     * is used.
68→     */
69→    public val propertyMapper: ((Property?, CodeStyleValue) -> T?)? = null,
70→
71→    /**
72→     * Custom function that represents [T] as String. Defaults to the standard `toString()` call. Override the
73→     * default implementation in case you need a different behavior than the standard `toString()` (e.g. for
74→     * collections joinToString() is more applicable).
75→     */
76→    public val propertyWriter: (T) -> String = { it.toString() },
77→
78→    /**
79→     * Optional message to be displayed whenever the value of the property is being retrieved while it has been deprecated.
80→     */
81→    val deprecationWarning: String? = null,
82→
83→    /**
84→     * Optional message to be displayed whenever the value of the property is being retrieved while it has been deprecated.
85→     */
86→    val deprecationError: String? = null,
87→
88→    /**
89→     * Name of the property. A property must be named in case multiple properties are defined for the same type.
90→     * Defaults to the name of the type when not set.
91→     */
92→    public val name: String = type.name,
93→)
94→

conversation_history.content:
1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  5→import mu.KotlinLogging
  6→import org.ec4j.core.model.Property
  7→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
  8→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValue
  9→
 10→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * Loaded [Property]s from `.editorconfig` files.
 14→ */
 15→public data class EditorConfig(
 16→    private val properties: Map<String, Property> = emptyMap(),
 17→) {
 18→    public constructor(vararg properties: Property) : this(
 19→        properties.associateBy { property -> property.name },
 20→    )
 21→
 22→    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue
 23→        get() =
 24→            with(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY) {
 25→                // Prevent printing a lot of warnings when the code_style_value is not defined explicitly by not using the function to get
 26→                // the default value for the property
 27→                type
 28→                    .getPropertyValue(type.name)
 29→                    .parsed
 30→                    ?: defaultValue
 31→            }
 32→
 33→    /**
 34→     * Gets the value of [editorConfigProperty] from [EditorConfig] provided that the name of the property is identical to the name of the
 35→     * type of the property. If the value is not found, the default value for the matching code style is returned.
 36→     */
 37→    public operator fun <T> get(editorConfigProperty: EditorConfigProperty<T>): T {
 38→        when {
 39→            editorConfigProperty.deprecationError != null ->
 40→                throw DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(
 41→                    "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is disallowed: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationError}",
 42→                )
 43→
 44→            editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning != null ->
 45→                LOGGER.warn { "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is deprecated: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning}" }
 46→        }
 47→        val property = properties.getOrElse(editorConfigProperty.name) {
 48→            throw IllegalStateException(
 49→                "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' can not be retrieved from this EditorConfig. Note that the EditorConfig which " +
 50→                    "is provided to class '${Rule::class.qualifiedName}' only contains the properties which are defined in the property " +
 51→                    "'${Rule::usesEditorConfigProperties.name}'.",
 52→            )
 53→        }
 54→
 55→        editorConfigProperty
 56→            .propertyMapper
 57→            ?.invoke(property, codeStyle)
 58→            ?.let { newValue ->
 59→                // If the property value is remapped to a non-null value then return it immediately.
 60→                val originalValue = property.sourceValue
 61→                if (newValue.toString() != originalValue) {
 62→                    LOGGER.trace {
 63→                        "Value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is remapped " +
 64→                            "from '$originalValue' to '$newValue'"
 65→                    }
 66→                }
 67→                return newValue
 68→            }
 69→
 70→        return if (property.isUnset) {
 71→            editorConfigProperty
 72→                .getDefaultValue()
 73→                .also {
 74→                    LOGGER.trace {
 75→                        "No value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' was found. Value has been defaulted to " +
 76→                            "'$it'. Setting the value explicitly in '.editorconfig' removes this message from the log."
 77→                    }
 78→                }
 79→        } else {
 80→            // In case the EditorConfig is loaded incorrectly it can happen that the type field of the loaded property is null. Getting the
 81→            // value via "property.getValueAs()" can lead to a class cast exception as the actual type of the value returned is transformed
 82→            // to a String instead of type T. Getting the value via the editorConfigProperty type is safe as it can not be null.
 83→            editorConfigProperty.getParsedValueOrDefault() ?: editorConfigProperty.getDefaultValue()
 84→        }
 85→    }
 86→
 87→    /**
 88→     * Gets the value of the property with [propertyType] and name [propertyName] from [EditorConfig]. Returns null if the type is not
 89→     * found. Also, returns null when no property with the name is found.
 90→     */
 91→    public fun <T> getEditorConfigValueOrNull(
 92→        propertyType: PropertyType<T>,
 93→        propertyName: String,
 94→    ): T? = propertyType.getPropertyValue(propertyName).parsed
 95→
 96→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getDefaultValue() =
 97→        when (codeStyle) {
 98→            CodeStyleValue.android -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
 99→            CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
100→            CodeStyleValue.official -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
101→            CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
102→            CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue
103→        }
104→
105→    /**
106→     * Checks whether a property with name [propertyName] is defined.
107→     */
108→    public fun contains(propertyName: String): Boolean = properties.contains(propertyName)
109→
110→    /**
111→     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType]. Returns null if the property type is not found. Can only be used for
112→     * properties for which the name of the property is identical to the name of the type.
113→     */
114→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(): T? =
115→        this.type.getParsedValueOrDefault(this.type.name, this.defaultValue)
116→
117→    /**
118→     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType] and name [propertyName]. Returns null if the property type is not
119→     * found. Intended to be used for properties for which the name is not identical to the name of the property type.
120→     */
121→    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(
122→        propertyName: String,
123→        defaultValue: T,
124→    ): T =
125→        getPropertyValue(propertyName)
126→            .let { propertyValue ->
127→                if (propertyValue.isValid) {
128→                    propertyValue.parsed
129→                } else {
130→                    LOGGER.warn {
131→                        "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' contains an invalid value '${propertyValue.source}'. The default value " +
132→                            "'$defaultValue' is used instead."
133→                    }
134→                    defaultValue
135→                }
136→            }
137→
138→    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getPropertyValue(propertyName: String): PropertyValue<T> =
139→        parse(
140→            properties[propertyName]?.sourceValue,
141→        )
142→
143→    /**
144→     * Maps all properties with given [mapper] to a collection.
145→     */
146→    public fun <T> map(mapper: (Property) -> T): Collection<T> =
147→        properties
148→            .values
149→            .map { mapper(it) }
150→
151→    /**
152→     * Adds the given [EditorConfigProperty]'s with its default value for the active code style to the [EditorConfig] if the property does
153→     * not yet exist.
154→     */
155→    public fun addPropertiesWithDefaultValueIfMissing(vararg defaultEditorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperty<*>): EditorConfig {
156→        val editorConfigProperties = defaultEditorConfigProperties.toSet()
157→        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
158→        return editorConfigProperties
159→            .defaultProperties()
160→            .plus(
161→                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
162→                properties,
163→            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
164→    }
165→
166→    /**
167→     * Creates an [EditorConfig] containing [editorConfigProperties] only. Retains the value of the property when defined in the original
168→     * [EditorConfig]. If the property does not exist in the original [EditorConfig], it will be added and its value is set to the default
169→     * value of the active code style.
170→     */
171→    public fun filterBy(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>): EditorConfig {
172→        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
173→        return editorConfigProperties
174→            .defaultProperties()
175→            .plus(
176→                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
177→                properties.filterBy(editorConfigProperties.map { it.name }),
178→            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
179→    }
180→
181→    private fun requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>) {
182→        val editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities =
183→            editorConfigProperties
184→                .groupBy { it.name }
185→                .filterValues { groupedEditorConfigProperties ->
186→                    groupedEditorConfigProperties
187→                        .distinctBy { it.hashCode() }
188→                        .count() > 1
189→                }
190→        require(editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities.isEmpty()) {
191→            editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities
192→                .map { (name, editorConfigProperties) ->
193→                    editorConfigProperties
194→                        .joinToString(
195→                            separator = "\n",
196→                            prefix = "Found multiple editorconfig properties with name '$name' but having distinct identities:\n",
197→                        ) { "  - $it" }
198→                }.joinToString(separator = "\n")
199→        }
200→    }
201→
202→    private fun Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>.defaultProperties(): Map<String, Property> {
203→        return associate { editorConfigProperty ->
204→            editorConfigProperty
205→                .writeDefaultValue()
206→                .let { editorConfigProperty.name to editorConfigProperty.toPropertyWithValue(it) }
207→        }
208→    }
209→
210→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.writeDefaultValue() = propertyWriter(getDefaultValue())
211→
212→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.toPropertyWithValue(value: String): Property =
213→        Property
214→            .builder()
215→            .type(type)
216→            .name(name)
217→            .value(value)
218→            .build()
219→
220→    private fun Map<String, Property>.filterBy(editorConfigProperties: List<String>): Map<String, Property> =
221→        filterKeys { it in editorConfigProperties }
222→}
223→
224→private class DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(message: String) : RuntimeException(message)
225→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.NoWildcardImportsRule.Companion.IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.LintViolation
  6→import org.junit.jupiter.api.Nested
  7→import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test
  8→
  9→class NoWildcardImportsRuleTest {
 10→    private val noWildcardImportsRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { NoWildcardImportsRule() }
 11→
 12→    @Nested
 13→    inner class `Given that editorconfig property packagesToUseImportOnDemandProperty is not set` {
 14→        @Test
 15→        fun `Wildcard imports are detected`() {
 16→            val code =
 17→                """
 18→                import a
 19→                import a.b.*
 20→                import a.b.c
 21→                import a.d.*
 22→                import foo.bar.`**`
 23→                """.trimIndent()
 24→            noWildcardImportsRuleAssertThat(code)
 25→                .hasLintViolationsWithoutAutoCorrect(
 26→                    LintViolation(2, 1, "Wildcard import"),
 27→                    LintViolation(4, 1, "Wildcard import"),
 28→                )
 29→        }
 30→
 31→        @Test
 32→        fun `Wildcard imports on packages which are accepted by IntelliJ Default are not detected`() {
 33→            val code =
 34→                """
 35→                import a.b
 36→                import kotlinx.android.synthetic.main.layout_name.*
 37→                """.trimIndent()
 38→            noWildcardImportsRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 39→        }
 40→    }
 41→
 42→    @Nested
 43→    inner class `Given that editorconfig property packagesToUseImportOnDemandProperty is set` {
 44→        @Test
 45→        fun `Given that the property is set with value 'unset' then packages which are accepted by IntelliJ Default are not detected`() {
 46→            val code =
 47→                """
 48→                import a.b
 49→                import kotlinx.android.synthetic.main.layout_name.*
 50→                import react.*
 51→                import react.dom.*
 52→                """.trimIndent()
 53→            noWildcardImportsRuleAssertThat(code)
 54→                .withEditorConfigOverride(IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND to "unset")
 55→                .hasLintViolationsWithoutAutoCorrect(
 56→                    LintViolation(3, 1, "Wildcard import"),
 57→                    LintViolation(4, 1, "Wildcard import"),
 58→                )
 59→        }
 60→
 61→        @Test
 62→        fun `Given that the property is set to some packages exclusive subpackages then wildcard imports for those directories are not detected`() {
 63→            val code =
 64→                """
 65→                import a.b
 66→                import kotlinx.android.synthetic.main.layout_name.*

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
 4→import mu.KotlinLogging
 5→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 6→
 7→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE = 100 // https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#line_wrapping
 8→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE = 140
 9→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG = "off"
10→
11→/**
12→ * Integer value that denotes that the property is to be considered as disabled.
13→ */
14→public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF: Int = Int.MAX_VALUE
15→
16→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
17→private var isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = false
18→
19→public val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Int> =
20→    EditorConfigProperty(
21→        name = PropertyType.max_line_length.name,
22→        type = PropertyType.max_line_length,
23→        defaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
24→        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE,
25→        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
26→        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE,
27→        propertyMapper = { property, codeStyleValue ->
28→            when {
29→                property == null || property.isUnset -> {
30→                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
31→                }
32→
33→                /**
34→                 * Internally, Ktlint uses integer 'Int.MAX_VALUE' to indicate that the max line length has to be ignored as this is easier
35→                 * in comparisons to check whether the maximum length of a line is exceeded.
36→                 */
37→                property.sourceValue == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
38→
39→                else ->
40→                    PropertyType
41→                        .max_line_length
42→                        .parse(property.sourceValue)
43→                        .let {
44→                            if (!it.isValid) {
45→                                if (!isInvalidValueLoggedBefore) {
46→                                    isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = true
47→                                    LOGGER.warn { "Found invalid '.editorconfig' property value: ${it.errorMessage}" }
48→                                }
49→                                if (it.source == "-1") {
50→                                    MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
51→                                } else {
52→                                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
53→                                }
54→                            } else {
55→                                it.parsed
56→                            }
57→                        }
58→            }
59→        },
60→        propertyWriter = { property ->
61→            if (property <= 0 || property == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
62→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG
63→            } else {
64→                property.toString()
65→            }
66→        },
67→    )
68→
69→private fun CodeStyleValue.defaultValue() =
70→    when (this) {
71→        CodeStyleValue.android -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE
72→        CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE
73→        CodeStyleValue.official -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
74→        CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
75→        CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE
76→    }
77→

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. The `NoWildcardImportsRule` has a default value for allowed wildcard imports (`java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**`) that applies to all code styles. The task is to clear this default for the `ktlint_official` code style.

Let me implement the fix:

function.name: edit
function.arguments:
{"file_path":"/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoWildcardImportsRule.kt","old_string":"public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
            EditorConfigProperty(
                type = PropertyType(
                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
                ),
                /**
                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
                 * it's subpackages.
                 *
                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
                 */
                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
            )","new_string":"public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
            EditorConfigProperty(
                type = PropertyType(
                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
                ),
                /**
                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
                 * it's subpackages.
                 *
                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
                 */
                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports(""),
                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
            )"}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.importordering.PatternEntry
  9→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
 12→
 13→public class NoWildcardImportsRule :
 14→    StandardRule(
 15→        id = "no-wildcard-imports",
 16→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 17→            IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND,
 18→        ),
 19→    ) {
 20→    private lateinit var allowedWildcardImports: List<PatternEntry>
 21→
 22→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 23→        allowedWildcardImports = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND]
 24→    }
 25→
 26→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 27→        node: ASTNode,
 28→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 29→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 30→    ) {
 31→        if (node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
 32→            val importDirective = node.psi as KtImportDirective
 33→            val path = importDirective.importPath ?: return
 34→            if (!path.isAllUnder) return
 35→            if (allowedWildcardImports.none { it.matches(path) }) {
 36→                emit(node.startOffset, "Wildcard import", false)
 37→            }
 38→        }
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    public companion object {
 42→        private const val WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES = "*"
 43→        private const val WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES = "**"
 44→
 45→        private fun parseAllowedWildcardImports(allowedWildcardImports: String): List<PatternEntry> {
 46→            val importsList = allowedWildcardImports.split(",").onEach { it.trim() }
 47→
 48→            return importsList.map { import ->
 49→                if (import.endsWith(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES)) { // java.**
 50→                    PatternEntry(
 51→                        packageName = import.removeSuffix(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES).plus(WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES),
 52→                        withSubpackages = true,
 53→                        hasAlias = false,
 54→                    )
 55→                } else {
 56→                    PatternEntry(
 57→                        packageName = import,
 58→                        withSubpackages = false,
 59→                        hasAlias = false,
 60→                    )
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        private val PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
 66→            { _, value ->
 67→                when {
 68→                    else -> try {
 69→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
 70→                            value,
 71→                            value?.let(Companion::parseAllowedWildcardImports) ?: emptyList(),
 72→                        )
 73→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 74→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
 75→                            value,
 76→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
 77→                        )
 78→                    }
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→
 82→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
 83→            EditorConfigProperty(
 84→                type = PropertyType(
 85→                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
 86→                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
 87→                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
 88→                ),
 89→                /**
 90→                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
 91→                 * it's subpackages.
 92→                 *
 93→                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
 94→                 */
 95→                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
 96→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
 97→            )
 98→    }
 99→}
100→
101→public val NO_WILDCARD_IMPORTS_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoWildcardImportsRule().ruleId

CHANGELOG.md:41:The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
CHANGELOG.md-42-
CHANGELOG.md-43-The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
CHANGELOG.md-44-
CHANGELOG.md-45-Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
CHANGELOG.md-46-
CHANGELOG.md-47-#### Package restructuring and class relocation
CHANGELOG.md-48-
CHANGELOG.md-49-The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
CHANGELOG.md-50-
CHANGELOG.md-51-This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.

0→# Changelog
 1→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 2→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 3→
 4→## Unreleased
 5→
 6→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 7→
 8→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 9→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
10→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
11→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
12→
13→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
14→
15→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
16→
17→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
18→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
19→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
20→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
21→
22→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
23→
24→#### Experimental rules
25→
26→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
27→
28→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
29→
30→#### EditorConfig
31→
32→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
33→
34→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
35→
36→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
37→
38→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
39→
40→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
41→
42→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
43→
44→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
45→
46→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
47→
48→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
49→
