1→## Annotation formatting
  2→
  3→Multiple annotations should be on a separate line than the annotated declaration; annotations with parameters should each be on separate lines; annotations should be followed by a space
  4→
  5→Rule-id: `annotation`
  6→
  7→## Argument list wrapping
  8→
  9→Rule-id: `argument-list-wrapping`
 10→
 11→## Chain wrapping
 12→
 13→When wrapping chained calls `.`, `?.` and `?:` should be placed on the next line
 14→
 15→Rule id: `chain-wrapping`
 16→
 17→## Enum entry
 18→
 19→Enum entry names should be uppercase underscore-separated names.
 20→
 21→Rule id: `enum-entry-name-case`
 22→
 23→## File name
 24→
 25→Files containing only one toplevel domain should be named according to that element.
 26→
 27→Rule id: `filename`
 28→
 29→## Final newline
 30→
 31→Ensures consistent usage of a newline at the end of each file. 
 32→
 33→This rule can be configured with `.editorconfig` property [`insert_final_newline`](../configuration-ktlint/#final-newline).
 34→
 35→Rule id: `final-newline`
 36→
 37→## Import ordering
 38→
 39→Imports ordered consistently (see [Custom ktlint EditorConfig properties](#custom-ktlint-specific-editorconfig-properties) for more)
 40→
 41→Rule id: `import-ordering`
 42→
 43→## Indentation
 44→
 45→Indentation formatting - respects `.editorconfig` `indent_size` with no continuation indent (see [EditorConfig](../configuration-ktlint/) section for more).
 46→
 47→Rule id: `indent`
 48→
 49→## Max line length
 50→
 51→Ensures that lines do not exceed the given length of `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` (see [EditorConfig](../configuration-ktlint/) section for more). This rule does not apply in a number of situations. For example, in the case a line exceeds the maximum line length due to and comment that disables ktlint rules than that comment is being ignored when validating the length of the line. The `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier` can be set to ignore identifiers which are enclosed in backticks, which for example is very useful when you want to allow longer names for unit tests.
 52→
 53→Rule id: `max-line-length`
 54→
 55→## Modifier order
 56→
 57→Consistent order of modifiers
 58→
 59→Rule id: `modifier-order`
 60→
 61→## Multiline if-else
 62→
 63→Braces required for multiline if/else statements.
 64→
 65→Rule id: `multiline-if-else`
 66→
 67→## No blank lines before `}`
 68→
 69→No blank lines before `}`.
 70→
 71→Rule id: `no-blank-line-before-rbrace`
 72→
 73→## No blank lines in chained method calls
 74→
 75→Rule id: `no-blank-lines-in-chained-method-calls`
 76→
 77→## No consecutive blank lines
 78→ 
 79→Rule id: `no-consecutive-blank-lines`
 80→
 81→## No empty (`{}`) class bodies
 82→
 83→Rule id: `no-empty-class-body`
 84→
 85→## No leading empty lines in method blocks
 86→ 
 87→Rule id: `no-empty-first-line-in-method-block`
 88→
 89→## No line break after else
 90→
 91→Disallows line breaks after the else keyword if that could lead to confusion, for example:
 92→```kotlin
 93→if (conditionA()) {
 94→    doSomething()
 95→} else
 96→if (conditionB()) {
 97→    doAnotherThing()
 98→}
 99→```
100→

CHANGELOG.md-809-- Add experimental rules for unnecessary spacing between modifiers in and after the last modifier in a modifier list ([#1361](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1361))
CHANGELOG.md-810-- New experimental rule for aligning the initial stars in a block comment when present (`experimental:block-comment-initial-star-alignment` ([#297](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/297))
CHANGELOG.md:811:- Respect `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` (`no-wildcard-imports`) ([#1272](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1272))
CHANGELOG.md-812-- Add new experimental rules for wrapping of block comment (`comment-wrapping`) ([#1403](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1403))
CHANGELOG.md-813-- Add new experimental rules for wrapping of KDoc comment (`kdoc-wrapping`) ([#1403](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1403))
CHANGELOG.md-814-- Add experimental rule for incorrect spacing after a type parameter list (`type-parameter-list-spacing`) ([#1366](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1366))
CHANGELOG.md-815-- Expand check task to run tests on JDK 17 - "testOnJdk17"
CHANGELOG.md-816-
CHANGELOG.md-817-### Fixed
CHANGELOG.md-818-- Fix lint message to "Unnecessary long whitespace" (`no-multi-spaces`) ([#1394](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1394))
CHANGELOG.md-819-- Do not remove trailing comma after a parameter of type array in an annotation (experimental:trailing-comma) ([#1379](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1379))
CHANGELOG.md-820-- Do not delete blank lines in KDoc (no-trailing-spaces) ([#1376](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1376))
CHANGELOG.md-821-- Do not indent raw string literals that are not followed by either trimIndent() or trimMargin() (`indent`) ([#1375](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1375))

 13→When wrapping chained calls `.`, `?.` and `?:` should be placed on the next line

 17→## Enum entry

 20→
 21→Rule id: `enum-entry-name-case`

 23→## File name

 25→Files containing only one toplevel domain should be named according to that element.

For example, in the case a line exceeds the maximum line length due to and comment that disables ktlint rules than that comment is being ignored when validating the length of the line. The `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier` can be set to ignore identifiers which are enclosed in backticks, which for example is very useful when you want to allow longer names for unit tests.

 76→
 77→## No consecutive blank lines
 78→ 
 79→Rule id: `no-consecutive-blank-lines`
 80→
 81→## No empty (`{}`) class bodies
 82→
 83→Rule id: `no-empty-class-body`
 84→
 85→## No leading empty lines in method blocks
 86→ 
 87→Rule id: `no-empty-first-line-in-method-block`
 88→
 89→## No line break after else
 90→
 91→Disallows line breaks after the else keyword if that could lead to confusion, for example:
 92→```kotlin
 93→if (conditionA()) {
 94→    doSomething()
 95→} else
 96→if (conditionB()) {
 97→    doAnotherThing()
 98→}

100→
101→Rule id: `no-line-break-after-else`
102→
103→## No line break before assignment 
104→

108→
109→## No multi spaces

113→Rule id: `no-multi-spaces`

134→    ```

137→    ```kotlin

139→    ```

142→
143→## No unused imports
144→
145→Rule id: `no-unused-imports`
146→
147→## No wildcard imports
148→

150→
151→!!! warning
152→
153→    In case property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` is not explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior. To disallow *all* wildcard imports, add property below to your `.editorconfig`:
154→    ```editorconfig
155→    [*.{kt,kts}]
156→    ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand = nothing
157→    ```
158→
159→Rule id: `no-wildcard-imports`

161→## Package name
162→
163→Validates that the package name matches the regular expression `[a-z][a-zA-Z\d]*(\.[a-z][a-zA-Z\d]*)*`.
164→
165→Rule id: `package-name`

167→## Parameter list wrapping
168→
169→When class/function signature doesn't fit on a single line, each parameter must be on a separate line
170→
171→Rule id: `parameter-list-wrapping`
172→
173→## Parameter wrapping
174→
175→When a function or class parameter doesn't fit on a single line, wrap the type or value to a separate line
176→
177→Rule id: `parameter-wrapping`
178→
179→## Property wrapping
180→
181→When a property doesn't fit on a single line, wrap the type or value to a separate line
182→
183→Rule id: `property-wrapping`
184→
185→## String template
186→
187→Consistent string templates (`$v` instead of `${v}`, `${p.v}` instead of `${p.v.toString()}`)
188→
189→Rule id: `string-template`
190→
191→## Trailing comma on call site
192→
193→Consistent removal (default) or adding of trailing comma's on call site.
194→
195→!!! important
196→    KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site` to configure the rule. When this property is enabled, KtLint *enforces* the usage of the trailing comma at call site while IntelliJ IDEA default formatter only *allows* to use the trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
197→
198→!!! note
199→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on call site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
200→    
201→    Although the [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
202→    
203→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
204→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
205→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
206→
207→!!! note
208→    Trailing comma on call site is automatically disabled if the [Wrapping](#wrapping) rule (or, before version `0.45.0`, the [Indentation](#indentation) rule) is disabled or not loaded. Because it cannot provide proper formatting with unwrapped calls. (see [dependencies](./dependencies.md)).
209→
210→    === "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
211→
212→        ```kotlin
213→        FooWrapper(
214→            Foo(
215→                a = 3,
216→                b = 4,
217→            ),
218→        )
219→        ```
220→    === "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
221→
222→        ```kotlin
223→        FooWrapper(Foo(
224→            a = 3,
225→            b = 4,
226→        ),) // it's weird to insert "," between unwrapped (continued) parenthesis
227→        ```
228→
229→
230→Rule id: `trailing-comma-on-call-site`
231→
232→## Trailing comma on declaration site
233→
234→Consistent removal (default) or adding of trailing comma's on declaration site.
235→
236→!!! important
237→    KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma` to configure the rule. When this property is enabled, KtLint *enforces* the usage of the trailing comma at declaration site while IntelliJ IDEA default formatter only *allows* to use the trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
238→
239→!!! note
240→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on declaration site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
241→
242→    The [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) encourages the usage of trailing comma's on the declaration site, but leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site. But next to this, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
243→    
244→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
245→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
246→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
247→
248→!!! note
249→    Trailing comma on declaration site is automatically disabled if the [Wrapping](#wrapping) rule (or, before version `0.45.0`, the [Indentation](#indentation) rule) is disabled or not loaded. Because it cannot provide proper formatting with unwrapped declarations. (see [dependencies](./dependencies.md)).
250→
251→    === "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
252→
253→        ```kotlin
254→        class FooWrapper(
255→            val foo = Foo(
256→                a = 3,
257→                b = 4,
258→            ),
259→        )
260→        ```
261→    === "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
262→
263→        ```kotlin
264→        class FooWrapper(val foo = Foo(
265→            a = 3,
266→            b = 4,
267→        ),) // it's weird to insert "," between unwrapped (continued) parenthesis
268→        ```
269→
270→
271→Rule id: `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site`
272→
273→## Wrapping
274→
275→Inserts missing newlines (for example between parentheses of a multi-line function call).
276→
277→Rule id: `wrapping`
278→
279→## Spacing
280→
281→### Annotation spacing
282→
283→Annotations should be separated by a single line break.
284→
285→Rule id: `annotation-spacing`
286→
287→### Colon spacing
288→
289→Consistent spacing around colon.
290→
291→Rule id: `colon-spacing`
292→
293→### Comma spacing
294→
295→Consistent spacing around comma.
296→
297→Rule id: `comma-spacing`
298→
299→### Comment spacing
300→
301→The end of line comment sign `//` should be preceded and followed by exactly a space.
302→
303→Rule id: `comment-spacing`
304→
305→### Curly spacing
306→
307→Consistent spacing around curly braces.
308→
309→Rule id: `curly-spacing`
310→
311→### Dot spacing
312→
313→Consistent spacing around dots.
314→
315→Rule id: `dot-spacing`
316→
317→### Double colon spacing
318→
319→No spaces around `::`.
320→
321→Rule id: `double-colon-spacing`
322→
323→### Keyword spacing
324→
325→Consistent spacing around keywords.
326→
327→Rule id: `keyword-spacing`
328→
329→### Operator spacing
330→
331→Consistent spacing around operators.
332→
333→Rule id: `op-spacing`
334→
335→### Parenthesis spacing
336→
337→Consistent spacing around parenthesis.
338→
339→Rule id: `paren-spacing`
340→
341→### Range spacing
342→
343→Consistent spacing around range operators.
344→
345→Rule id: `range-spacing`
346→
347→### Angle bracket spacing
348→
349→No spaces around angle brackets when used for typing.
350→
351→Rule id: `spacing-around-angle-brackets`
352→
353→### Blank line between declarations with annotations
354→
355→Declarations with annotations should be separated by a blank line.
356→
357→Rule id: `spacing-between-declarations-with-annotations`
358→
359→### Blank line between declaration with comments
360→
361→Declarations with comments should be separated by a blank line.
362→
363→Rule id: `spacing-between-declarations-with-comments`
364→
365→### Unary operator spacing
366→
367→No spaces around unary operators.
368→
369→Rule id: `unary-op-spacing`
370→

{"file_path":"/project/docs/rules/standard.md","content":"## Annotation formatting

Multiple annotations should be on a separate line than the annotated declaration; annotations with parameters should each be on separate lines; annotations should be followed by a space

Rule-id: `annotation`

## Argument list wrapping

Rule-id: `argument-list-wrapping`

## Chain wrapping

When wrapping chained calls `.`, `?.` and `?:` should be placed on the next line

Rule id: `chain-wrapping`

## Enum entry

Enum entry names should be uppercase underscore-separated names.

Rule id: `enum-entry-name-case`

## File name

Files containing only one toplevel domain should be named according to that element.

Rule id: `filename`

## Final newline

Ensures consistent usage of a newline at the end of each file. 

This rule can be configured with `.editorconfig` property [`insert_final_newline`](../configuration-ktlint/#final-newline).

Rule id: `final-newline`

## Import ordering

Imports ordered consistently (see [Custom ktlint EditorConfig properties](#custom-ktlint-specific-editorconfig-properties) for more)

Rule id: `import-ordering`

## Indentation

Indentation formatting - respects `.editorconfig` `indent_size` with no continuation indent (see [EditorConfig](../configuration-ktlint/) section for more).

Rule id: `indent`

## Max line length

Ensures that lines do not exceed the given length of `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` (see [EditorConfig](../configuration-ktlint/) section for more). This rule does not apply in a number of situations. For example, in the case a line exceeds the maximum line length due to and comment that disables ktlint rules than that comment is being ignored when validating the length of the line. The `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier` can be set to ignore identifiers which are enclosed in backticks, which for example is very useful when you want to allow longer names for unit tests.

Rule id: `max-line-length`

## Modifier order

Consistent order of modifiers

Rule id: `modifier-order`

## Multiline if-else

Braces required for multiline if/else statements.

Rule id: `multiline-if-else`

## No blank lines before `}`

No blank lines before `}`.

Rule id: `no-blank-line-before-rbrace`

## No blank lines in chained method calls

Rule id: `no-blank-lines-in-chained-method-calls`

## No consecutive blank lines
 
Rule id: `no-consecutive-blank-lines`

## No empty (`{}`) class bodies

Rule id: `no-empty-class-body`

## No leading empty lines in method blocks
 
Rule id: `no-empty-first-line-in-method-block`

## No line break after else

Disallows line breaks after the else keyword if that could lead to confusion, for example:
```kotlin
if (conditionA()) {
    doSomething()
} else
if (conditionB()) {
    doAnotherThing()
}
```

Rule id: `no-line-break-after-else`

## No line break before assignment 

When a line is broken at an assignment (`=`) operator the break comes after the symbol.

Rule id: `no-line-break-before-assignment`

## No multi spaces

Except in indentation and in KDoc's it is not allowed to have multiple consecutive spaces.

Rule id: `no-multi-spaces`

## No semicolons

No semicolons (unless used to separate multiple statements on the same line).

Rule id: `no-semi`

## No trailing whitespaces

Rule id: `no-trailing-spaces`

## No `Unit` as return type 

The `Unit` type is not allowed as return type of a function.
returns (`fun fn {}` instead of `fun fn: Unit {}`)

=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"

    ```kotlin
    fun fn() {}
    ```
=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"

    ```kotlin
    fun fn(): Unit {}
    ```

Rule id: `no-unit-return`

## No unused imports

Rule id: `no-unused-imports`

## No wildcard imports

No wildcard imports except imports listed in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`.

!!! warning

    In case property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` is not explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior. To disallow *all* wildcard imports, add property below to your `.editorconfig`:
    ```editorconfig
    [*.{kt,kts}]
    ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand = nothing
    ```
    
    The `ktlint_official` code style does not allow any wildcard imports by default.

Rule id: `no-wildcard-imports`

## Package name

Validates that the package name matches the regular expression `[a-z][a-zA-Z\d]*(\.[a-z][a-zA-Z\d]*)*`.

Rule id: `package-name`

## Parameter list wrapping

When class/function signature doesn't fit on a single line, each parameter must be on a separate line

Rule id: `parameter-list-wrapping`

## Parameter wrapping

When a function or class parameter doesn't fit on a single line, wrap the type or value to a separate line

Rule id: `parameter-wrapping`

## Property wrapping

When a property doesn't fit on a single line, wrap the type or value to a separate line

Rule id: `property-wrapping`

## String template

Consistent string templates (`$v` instead of `${v}`, `${p.v}` instead of `${p.v.toString()}`)

Rule id: `string-template`

## Trailing comma on call site

Consistent removal (default) or adding of trailing comma's on call site.

!!! important
    KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site` to configure the rule. When this property is enabled, KtLint *enforces* the usage of the trailing comma at call site while IntelliJ IDEA default formatter only *allows* to use the trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.

!!! note
    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on call site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
    
    Although the [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
    
     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.

!!! note
    Trailing comma on call site is automatically disabled if the [Wrapping](#wrapping) rule (or, before version `0.45.0`, the [Indentation](#indentation) rule) is disabled or not loaded. Because it cannot provide proper formatting with unwrapped calls. (see [dependencies](./dependencies.md)).

    === "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"

        ```kotlin
        FooWrapper(
            Foo(
                a = 3,
                b = 4,
            ),
        )
        ```
    === "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"

        ```kotlin
        FooWrapper(Foo(
            a = 3,
            b = 4,
        ),) // it's weird to insert "," between unwrapped (continued) parenthesis
        ```


Rule id: `trailing-comma-on-call-site`

## Trailing comma on declaration site

Consistent removal (default) or adding of trailing comma's on declaration site.

!!! important
    KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma` to configure the rule. When this property is enabled, KtLint *enforces* the usage of the trailing comma at declaration site while IntelliJ IDEA default formatter only *allows* to use the trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.

!!! note
    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on declaration site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.

    The [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) encourages the usage of trailing comma's on the declaration site, but leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site. But next to this, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
    
     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.

!!! note
    Trailing comma on declaration site is automatically disabled if the [Wrapping](#wrapping) rule (or, before version `0.45.0`, the [Indentation](#indentation) rule) is disabled or not loaded. Because it cannot provide proper formatting with unwrapped declarations. (see [dependencies](./dependencies.md)).

    === "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"

        ```kotlin
        class FooWrapper(
            val foo = Foo(
                a = 3,
                b = 4,
            ),
        )
        ```
    === "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"

        ```kotlin
        class FooWrapper(val foo = Foo(
            a = 3,
            b = 4,
        ),) // it's weird to insert "," between unwrapped (continued) parenthesis
        ```


Rule id: `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site`

## Wrapping

Inserts missing newlines (for example between parentheses of a multi-line function call).

Rule id: `wrapping`

## Spacing

### Annotation spacing

Annotations should be separated by a single line break.

Rule id: `annotation-spacing`

### Colon spacing

Consistent spacing around colon.

Rule id: `colon-spacing`

### Comma spacing

Consistent spacing around comma.

Rule id: `comma-spacing`

### Comment spacing

The end of line comment sign `//` should be preceded and followed by exactly a space.

Rule id: `comment-spacing`

### Curly spacing

Consistent spacing around curly braces.

Rule id: `curly-spacing`

### Dot spacing

Consistent spacing around dots.

Rule id: `dot-spacing`

### Double colon spacing

No spaces around `::`.

Rule id: `double-colon-spacing`

### Keyword spacing

Consistent spacing around keywords.

Rule id: `keyword-spacing`

### Operator spacing

Consistent spacing around operators.

Rule id: `op-spacing`

### Parenthesis spacing

Consistent spacing around parenthesis.

Rule id: `paren-spacing`

### Range spacing

Consistent spacing around range operators.

Rule id: `range-spacing`

### Angle bracket spacing

No spaces around angle brackets when used for typing.

Rule id: `spacing-around-angle-brackets`

### Blank line between declarations with annotations

Declarations with annotations should be separated by a blank line.

Rule id: `spacing-between-declarations-with-annotations`

### Blank line between declaration with comments

Declarations with comments should be separated by a blank line.

Rule id: `spacing-between-declarations-with-comments`

### Unary operator spacing

No spaces around unary operators.

Rule id: `unary-op-spacing`"}

function.arguments:
{"file_path":"/project/docs/faq.md","content":"# FAQ

## Is there a built-in way of ignoring lines?

Yes, ktlint supports the standard Kotlin syntax for suppressing warnings:

```kotlin
file.kt
fun foo() { // ktlint-disable
    // some poorly formatted code that ktlint should not check
} // ktlint-enable
```

You can also disable a specific rule:

```kotlin
file.kt
fun foo() { // ktlint-disable no-wildcard-imports
    import java.util.*
} // ktlint-enable no-wildcard-imports
```

Note that for the `ktlint-disable` comment to work, no code has to be placed before it on the same line.

You can also suppress rules for an entire file by placing the comment at the very top of the file. For example:

```kotlin
file.kt
// ktlint-disable no-wildcard-imports
import java.util.*
```

Also, you can suppress ktlint warnings via Kotlin annotations:

```kotlin
@Suppress("ktlint:no-wildcard-imports")
import java.util.*
```

In the future, when moving to a custom rule set (i.e., rules beyond this list), you will include the rule set name: e.g.:

```kotlin
@Suppress("ktlint:my-custom-ruleset:my-lint")
```

## Can I check only a subset of rules?

Yes, you can disable rules via the `.editorconfig` file:

```ktlint
[*.{kt,kts}]
ktlint_disabled_rules = experimental,experimental:rule-id-1
# or
disabled_rules = experimental:rule-id-1,standard:rule-id-2
```

Disabled rules can be re-enabled using the [EditorConfig property `ktlint_enabled_rules`](../configuration-ktlint.md#enabled-rules).

The standard and experimental rule sets will still be loaded by ktlint. However, the specified rules will be skipped while linting the code.

Make sure to include the correct rule id prefix (like `standard:` or `experimental:`). The prefix is displayed in the Ktlint command line output when a rule reports a violation.

!!! warning
    When you reference a rule, you typically need to include the rule set id prefix (e.g., `standard:no-wildcard-imports`). However, for backwards compatibility, when referencing a rule in `ktlint_disabled_rules` or `ktlint_enabled_rules`, the standard and experimental rule sets do not need to be prefixed.

!!! note
    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.

## Why is wildcard import `java.util.*` not reported by the `no-wildcard-imports` rule?

The `no-wildcard-imports` rule forbids wildcard imports, except for imports defined in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`. When the property is not explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior (unless you are using the `ktlint_official` code style, which does not allow any wildcard imports by default).

!!! note
    Property names like `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` are directly taken from IntelliJ IDEA's code style settings to make it easier to migrate the `.editorconfig` to ktlint. Ktlint will warn you if you use an editorconfig property that it does not recognize."}

docs/faq.md-67-    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
docs/faq.md-68-
docs/faq.md:69:## Why is wildcard import `java.util.*` not reported by the `no-wildcard-imports` rule?
docs/faq.md-70-
docs/faq.md-71-The `no-wildcard-imports` rule forbids wildcard imports, except for imports defined in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`. When the property is not explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior (unless you are using the `ktlint_official` code style, which does not allow any wildcard imports by default).
docs/faq.md-72-
docs/faq.md-73-!!! note
docs/faq.md-74-    Property names like `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` are directly taken from IntelliJ IDEA's code style settings to make it easier to migrate the `.editorconfig` to ktlint. Ktlint will warn you if you use an editorconfig property that it does not recognize.

 10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
 12→
 13→public class NoWildcardImportsRule :
 14→    StandardRule(
 15→        id = "no-wildcard-imports",
 16→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 17→            IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND,
 18→        ),
 19→    ) {
 20→    private lateinit var allowedWildcardImports: List<PatternEntry>
 21→
 22→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 23→        allowedWildcardImports = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND]
 24→    }
 25→
 26→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 27→        node: ASTNode,
 28→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 29→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 30→    ) {
 31→        if (node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
 32→            val importDirective = node.psi as KtImportDirective
 33→            val path = importDirective.importPath ?: return
 34→            if (!path.isAllUnder) return
 35→            if (allowedWildcardImports.none { it.matches(path) }) {
 36→                emit(node.startOffset, "Wildcard import", false)
 37→            }
 38→        }
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    public companion object {
 42→        private const val WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES = "*"
 43→        private const val WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES = "**"
 44→
 45→        private fun parseAllowedWildcardImports(allowedWildcardImports: String): List<PatternEntry> {
 46→            val importsList = allowedWildcardImports.split(",").onEach { it.trim() }
 47→
 48→            return importsList.map { import ->
 49→                if (import.endsWith(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES)) { // java.**
 50→                    PatternEntry(
 51→                        packageName = import.removeSuffix(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES).plus(WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES),
 52→                        withSubpackages = true,
 53→                        hasAlias = false,
 54→                    )
 55→                } else {
 56→                    PatternEntry(
 57→                        packageName = import,
 58→                        withSubpackages = false,
 59→                        hasAlias = false,
 60→                    )
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        private val PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
 66→            { _, value ->
 67→                when {
 68→                    else -> try {
 69→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
 70→                            value,
 71→                            value?.let(Companion::parseAllowedWildcardImports) ?: emptyList(),
 72→                        )
 73→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 74→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
 75→                            value,
 76→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
 77→                        )
 78→                    }
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→
 82→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
 83→            EditorConfigProperty(
 84→                type = PropertyType(
 85→                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
 86→                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
 87→                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
 88→                ),
 89→                /**
 90→                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
 91→                 * it's subpackages.
 92→                 *
 93→                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
 94→                 */
 95→                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
 96→                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports(""),
 97→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
 98→            )
 99→    }
100→}

content.text:
Perfect! I've successfully implemented the fix for removing the default allowed wildcard imports in the `ktlint_official` codestyle. Let me provide a summary of what was changed:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
The `ktlint_official` code style was inheriting the default value for `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` which allowed wildcard imports like `java.util.*` and `kotlinx.android.synthetic.**` to keep compatibility with IntelliJ IDEA. This was inconsistent with the goal of the `ktlint_official` style which should discourage wildcard imports.

### Solution
Added a `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` parameter to the `IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND` editor config property, setting it to an empty list, which effectively removes all default wildcard import exceptions for the `ktlint_official` code style.

### Files Modified

1. **`/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`**
   - Added `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("")` to the `EditorConfigProperty` definition on line 96
   - This ensures that when using `ktlint_official` code style, no wildcard imports are allowed by default

2. **`/project/docs/rules/standard.md`**
   - Added a note explaining that the `ktlint_official` code style does not allow any wildcard imports by default

3. **`/project/docs/faq.md`**
   - Updated the FAQ entry to include a note about the `ktlint_official` code style behavior regarding wildcard imports

### How It Works
The `EditorConfigProperty` class supports code style-specific default values. When a property is accessed, the `EditorConfig` class automatically selects the appropriate default based on the active code style:
- For `ktlint_official`: Uses an empty list (no wildcard imports allowed)
- For `intellij_idea` and `android_studio`: Uses the existing default (`java.util.*` and `kotlinx.android.synthetic.**`)

This maintains backward compatibility with IntelliJ IDEA for users of other code styles while enforcing stricter rules for `ktlint_official`.

101→public val NO_WILDCARD_IMPORTS_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoWildcardImportsRule().ruleId

 62→@Command(
 63→    headerHeading =
 64→    """
 65→An anti-bikeshedding Kotlin linter with built-in formatter.
 66→(https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint).
 67→
 68→Usage:
 69→  ktlint <flags> [patterns]
 70→  java -jar ktlint.jar <flags> [patterns]
 71→
 72→Examples:
 73→  # Check the style of all Kotlin files (ending with '.kt' or '.kts') inside the current dir (recursively).
 74→  #
 75→  # Hidden folders will be skipped.
 76→  ktlint
 77→
 78→  # Check only certain locations starting from the current directory.
 79→  #
 80→  # Prepend ! to negate the pattern, KtLint uses .gitignore pattern style syntax.
 81→  # Globs are applied starting from the last one.
 82→  #
 83→  # Hidden folders will be skipped.
 84→  # Check all '.kt' files in 'src/' directory, but ignore files ending with 'Test.kt':
 85→  ktlint "src/**/*.kt" "!src/**/*Test.kt"
 86→  # Check all '.kt' files in 'src/' directory, but ignore 'generated' directory and its subdirectories:
 87→  ktlint "src/**/*.kt" "!src/**/generated/**"
 88→
 89→  # Auto-correct style violations.
 90→  ktlint -F "src/**/*.kt"
 91→
 92→  # Using custom reporter jar and overriding report location
 93→  ktlint --reporter=csv,artifact=/path/to/reporter/csv.jar,output=my-custom-report.csv
 94→Flags:
 95→""",
 96→    synopsisHeading = "",
 97→    customSynopsis = [""],
 98→    sortOptions = false,
 99→    mixinStandardHelpOptions = true,
100→    versionProvider = KtlintVersionProvider::class,
101→)
102→internal class KtlintCommandLine {
103→    @CommandLine.Spec
104→    private lateinit var commandSpec: CommandLine.Model.CommandSpec
105→
106→    @Deprecated(message = "Marked for removal in KtLint 0.50")
107→    @Option(
108→        names = ["--android", "-a"],
109→        description = ["Turn on Android Kotlin Style Guide compatibility"],
110→        hidden = true,
111→    )
112→    var android: Boolean = false
113→
114→    @Option(
115→        // Ensure that the code-style can be set on sub commands and is visible in the help documentation
116→        scope = CommandLine.ScopeType.INHERIT,
117→        names = ["--code-style"],
118→        description = [
119→            "Defines the code style (ktlint_official, intellij_idea or android_studio) to be used for formatting the code. It is " +
120→                "advised to define '.editorconfig' property 'ktlint_code_style'.",
121→        ],
122→        converter = [CodeStyleValueConverter::class],
123→    )
124→    var codeStyle: CodeStyleValue? = null
125→
126→    @Option(
127→        names = ["--color"],
128→        description = ["Make output colorful"],
129→    )
130→    var color: Boolean = false
131→
132→    @Option(
133→        names = ["--color-name"],
134→        description = ["Customize the output color"],
135→    )
136→    var colorName: String = Color.DARK_GRAY.name
137→
138→    @Option(
139→        names = ["--disabled_rules"],
140→        description = [
141→            "Comma-separated list of rules to globally disable." +
142→                " To disable standard ktlint rule-set use --disabled_rules=standard",
143→        ],
144→    )
145→    var disabledRules: String = ""
146→
147→    // TODO: this should have been a command, not a flag (consider changing in 1.0.0)
148→    @Option(
149→        names = ["--format", "-F"],
150→        description = ["Fix any deviations from the code style"],
151→    )
152→    private var format: Boolean = false
153→
154→    @Option(
155→        names = ["--limit"],
156→        description = ["Maximum number of errors to show (default: show all)"],
157→    )
158→    private var limit: Int = -1
159→        get() = if (field < 0) Int.MAX_VALUE else field
160→
161→    @Option(
162→        names = ["--relative"],
163→        description = [
164→            "Print files relative to the working directory " +
165→                "(e.g. dir/file.kt instead of /home/user/project/dir/file.kt)",
166→        ],
167→    )
168→    var relative: Boolean = false
169→
170→    @Option(
171→        names = ["--reporter"],
172→        description = [
173→            "A reporter to use (built-in: plain (default), plain?group_by_file, plain-summary, json, sarif, " +
174→                "checkstyle, html). To use a third-party reporter specify a path to a JAR file on the filesystem " +
175→                "via ',artifact=' option. To override reporter output, use ',output=' option.",
176→        ],
177→    )
178→    private var reporterConfigurations: List<String> = ArrayList()
179→
180→    @Option(
181→        names = ["--ruleset", "-R"],
182→        description = ["A path to a JAR file containing additional ruleset(s)"],
183→    )
184→    var rulesetJarPaths: List<String> = ArrayList()
185→
186→    @Option(
187→        names = ["--stdin"],
188→        description = ["Read file from stdin"],
189→    )
190→    private var stdin: Boolean = false
191→
192→    @Option(
193→        names = ["--patterns-from-stdin"],
194→        description = [
195→            "Read additional patterns to check/format from stdin. " +
196→                "Patterns are delimited by the given argument. (default is newline) " +
197→                "If the argument is an empty string, the NUL byte is used.",
198→        ],
199→        arity = "0..1",
200→        fallbackValue = "\n",
201→    )
202→    private var stdinDelimiter: String? = null
203→
204→    @Option(
205→        names = ["--editorconfig"],
206→        description = [
207→            "Path to the default '.editorconfig'. A property value from this file is used only when no " +
208→                "'.editorconfig' file on the path to the source file specifies that property. Note: up until ktlint " +
209→                "0.46 the property value in this file used to override values found in '.editorconfig' files on the " +
210→                "path to the source file.",
211→        ],
212→    )
213→    private var editorConfigPath: String? = null
214→
215→    @Option(
216→        names = ["--experimental"],
217→        description = ["Enable experimental rules"],
218→    )
219→    var experimental: Boolean = false
220→
221→    @Option(
222→        names = ["--baseline"],
223→        description = ["Defines a baseline file to check against"],
224→    )
225→    private var baselinePath: String = ""
226→
227→    @Parameters(hidden = true)
228→    private var patterns = emptyList<String>()
229→
230→    @Option(
231→        names = ["--log-level", "-l"],
232→        description = ["Defines the minimum log level (trace, debug, info, warn, error) or none to suppress all logging"],
233→        converter = [LogLevelConverter::class],
234→    )
235→    private var minLogLevel: Level = Level.INFO
236→
237→    private val tripped = AtomicBoolean()
238→    private val fileNumber = AtomicInteger()
239→    private val errorNumber = AtomicInteger()
240→
241→    internal var debug: Boolean = false
242→        get() = Level.DEBUG.isGreaterOrEqual(minLogLevel)
243→        private set
244→
245→    private fun disabledRulesEditorConfigOverrides() =
246→        disabledRules
247→            .split(",")
248→            .filter { it.isNotBlank() }
249→            .map {
250→                // For backwards compatibility, prefix the rule id with the standard rule set id when missing
251→                RuleId.prefixWithStandardRuleSetIdWhenMissing(it)
252→            }.map { RuleId(it).createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.disabled }
253→            .toTypedArray()
254→
255→    fun run() {
256→        val editorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride
257→            .EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE
258→            .applyIf(experimental) {
259→                logger.debug { "Add editor config override to allow the experimental rule set" }
260→                plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)
261→            }.applyIf(disabledRules.isNotBlank()) {
262→                logger.debug { "Add editor config override to disable rules: '$disabledRules'" }
263→                plus(*disabledRulesEditorConfigOverrides())
264→            }.applyIf(android) {
265→                logger.debug { "Add editor config override to set code style to 'android'" }
266→                plus(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY to CodeStyleValue.android)
267→            }.applyIf(stdin) {
268→                logger.debug {
269→                    "Add editor config override to disable 'filename' rule which can not be used in combination with reading from <stdin>"
270→                }
271→                plus(FILENAME_RULE_ID.createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.disabled)
272→            }
273→
274→        if (android) {
275→            logger.error {
276→                "Option '--android' / '-a' is deprecated and replaced with option '--code-style=android_studio'. Setting '.editorconfig' " +
277→                    "property 'ktlint_code_style=android_studio' might be a better idea for a project that is always to formatted with " +
278→                    "this code style."
279→            }
280→        }
281→        assertStdinAndPatternsFromStdinOptionsMutuallyExclusive()
282→        patterns = patterns.replaceWithPatternsFromStdinOrDefaultPatternsWhenEmpty()
283→
284→        val start = System.currentTimeMillis()
285→
286→        val ruleProviders = ruleProviders()
287→        val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
288→            ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
289→            editorConfigDefaults = editorConfigDefaults(ruleProviders),
290→            editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride,
291→            isInvokedFromCli = true,
292→        )
293→
294→        val baseline = if (stdin || baselinePath.isBlank()) {
295→            Baseline(status = Baseline.Status.DISABLED)
296→        } else {
297→            loadBaseline(baselinePath)
298→        }
299→
300→        val aggregatedReporter = ReporterAggregator(
301→            baseline,
302→            reporterConfigurations,
303→            color,
304→            colorName,
305→            stdin,
306→            format,
307→            relative,
308→        ).aggregatedReporter()
309→
310→        aggregatedReporter.beforeAll()
311→        if (stdin) {
312→            lintStdin(
313→                ktLintRuleEngine,
314→                aggregatedReporter,
315→            )
316→        } else {
317→            lintFiles(
318→                ktLintRuleEngine,
319→                baseline.lintErrorsPerFile,
320→                aggregatedReporter,
321→            )
322→        }
323→        aggregatedReporter.afterAll()
324→
325→        logger.debug {
326→            "Finished processing in ${System.currentTimeMillis() - start}ms / $fileNumber file(s) scanned / $errorNumber error(s) found"
327→        }
328→        if (fileNumber.get() == 0) {
329→            // Do not return an error as this would implicate that in a multi-module project, each module has to contain
330→            // at least one kotlin file.
331→            logger.warn { "No files matched $patterns" }
332→        }
333→        if (tripped.get()) {
334→            exitKtLintProcess(1)
335→        } else {
336→            exitKtLintProcess(0)
337→        }
338→    }
339→
340→    private fun editorConfigDefaults(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigDefaults {
341→        val fullyExpandedEditorConfigPath =
342→            editorConfigPath
343→                ?.expandTildeToFullPath()
344→                ?.let { path -> Paths.get(path) }
345→        return EditorConfigDefaults.load(fullyExpandedEditorConfigPath, ruleProviders.propertyTypes())
346→    }
347→
348→    private fun List<String>.replaceWithPatternsFromStdinOrDefaultPatternsWhenEmpty(): List<String> {
349→        val localStdinDelimiter: String? = stdinDelimiter
350→        return when {
351→            localStdinDelimiter != null -> {
352→                val stdinPatterns: Set<String> = readPatternsFromStdin(localStdinDelimiter.ifEmpty { "\u0000" })
353→                if (isNotEmpty() && stdinPatterns.isNotEmpty()) {
354→                    logger.warn {
355→                        "Patterns specified at command line ($this) and patterns from 'stdin' due to flag '--patterns-from-stdin' " +
356→                            "($stdinPatterns) are merged"
357→                    }
358→                }
359→                // Note: it is okay in case both the original patterns and the patterns from stdin are empty
360→                this.plus(stdinPatterns)
361→            }
362→            this.isEmpty() -> {
363→                logger.info { "Enable default patterns $DEFAULT_PATTERNS" }
364→                DEFAULT_PATTERNS
365→            }
366→            else -> {
367→                // Keep original patterns
368→                this
369→            }
370→        }
371→    }
372→
373→    // Do not convert to "val" as the function depends on PicoCli options which are not fully instantiated until the "run" method is started
374→    internal fun ruleProviders(): Set<RuleProvider> = loadRuleProviders(rulesetJarPaths.toFilesURIList())
375→
376→    // Do not convert to "val" as the function depends on PicoCli options which are not fully instantiated until the "run" method is started
377→    internal fun configureLogger() {
378→        logger = KotlinLogging
379→            .logger {}
380→            .setDefaultLoggerModifier { logger ->
381→                (logger.underlyingLogger as Logger).level = minLogLevel
382→            }
383→            .initKtLintKLogger()
384→    }
385→
386→    private fun assertStdinAndPatternsFromStdinOptionsMutuallyExclusive() {
387→        if (stdin && stdinDelimiter != null) {
388→            throw ParameterException(
389→                commandSpec.commandLine(),
390→                "Options --stdin and --patterns-from-stdin are mutually exclusive",
391→            )
392→        }
393→    }
394→
395→    private fun lintFiles(
396→        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
397→        lintErrorsPerFile: Map<String, List<KtlintCliError>>,
398→        reporter: ReporterV2,
399→    ) {
400→        FileSystems
401→            .getDefault()
402→            .fileSequence(patterns)
403→            .map { it.toFile() }
404→            .takeWhile { errorNumber.get() < limit }
405→            .map { file ->
406→                val fileName = file.toPath().absolutePathString()
407→                Callable {
408→                    fileName to process(
409→                        ktLintRuleEngine = ktLintRuleEngine,
410→                        code = Code.fromFile(file),
411→                        baselineLintErrors = lintErrorsPerFile.getOrDefault(fileName, emptyList()),
412→                    )
413→                }
414→            }.parallel({ (fileName, errList) -> report(Paths.get(fileName).relativeRoute, errList, reporter) })
415→    }
416→
417→    private fun lintStdin(
418→        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
419→        reporter: ReporterV2,
420→    ) {
421→        report(
422→            KtLintRuleEngine.STDIN_FILE,
423→            process(
424→                ktLintRuleEngine = ktLintRuleEngine,
425→                code = Code.fromStdin(),
426→                baselineLintErrors = emptyList(),
427→            ),
428→            reporter,
429→        )
430→    }
431→
432→    private fun report(
433→        fileName: String,
434→        ktlintCliErrors: List<KtlintCliError>,
435→        reporter: ReporterV2,
436→    ) {
437→        fileNumber.incrementAndGet()
438→        val errListLimit = minOf(ktlintCliErrors.size, maxOf(limit - errorNumber.get(), 0))
439→        errorNumber.addAndGet(errListLimit)
440→
441→        val relativeRoute = Paths.get(fileName).relativeRoute
442→        reporter.before(relativeRoute)
443→        ktlintCliErrors
444→            .take(errListLimit)
445→            .forEach { reporter.onLintError(relativeRoute, it) }
446→        reporter.after(relativeRoute)
447→    }
448→
449→    private fun process(
450→        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
451→        code: Code,
452→        baselineLintErrors: List<KtlintCliError>,
453→    ): List<KtlintCliError> {
454→        if (code.fileName != null) {
455→            logger.trace { "Checking ${File(code.fileName!!).location(relative)}" }
456→        }
457→        val result = ArrayList<KtlintCliError>()
458→        if (format) {
459→            val formattedFileContent = try {
460→                ktLintRuleEngine.format(code) { lintError, corrected ->
461→                    val ktlintCliError = KtlintCliError(
462→                        line = lintError.line,
463→                        col = lintError.col,
464→                        ruleId = lintError.ruleId,
465→                        detail = lintError
466→                            .detail
467→                            .applyIf(corrected) { "$this (cannot be auto-corrected)" },
468→                        status = if (corrected) {
469→                            FORMAT_IS_AUTOCORRECTED
470→                        } else {
471→                            LINT_CAN_NOT_BE_AUTOCORRECTED
472→                        },
473→                    )
474→                    if (baselineLintErrors.doesNotContain(ktlintCliError)) {
475→                        result.add(ktlintCliError)
476→                        if (!corrected) {
477→                            tripped.set(true)
478→                        }
479→                    }
480→                }
481→            } catch (e: Exception) {
482→                result.add(e.toKtlintCliError(code))
483→                tripped.set(true)
484→                code.content // making sure `cat file | ktlint --stdin > file` is (relatively) safe
485→            }
486→            when {
487→                code.isStdIn -> print(formattedFileContent)
488→                code.content != formattedFileContent ->
489→                    code
490→                        .filePath
491→                        ?.toFile()
492→                        ?.writeText(formattedFileContent, charset("UTF-8"))
493→            }
494→        } else {
495→            try {
496→                ktLintRuleEngine.lint(code) { lintError ->
497→                    val ktlintCliError = KtlintCliError(
498→                        line = lintError.line,
499→                        col = lintError.col,
500→                        ruleId = lintError.ruleId,
501→                        detail = lintError.detail,
502→                        status = if (lintError.canBeAutoCorrected) {
503→                            LINT_CAN_BE_AUTOCORRECTED
504→                        } else {
505→                            LINT_CAN_NOT_BE_AUTOCORRECTED
506→                        },
507→                    )
508→                    if (baselineLintErrors.doesNotContain(ktlintCliError)) {
509→                        result.add(ktlintCliError)
510→                        tripped.set(true)
511→                    }
512→                }
513→            } catch (e: Exception) {
514→                result.add(e.toKtlintCliError(code))
515→                tripped.set(true)
516→            }
517→        }
518→        return result
519→    }
520→
521→    private fun Exception.toKtlintCliError(code: Code): KtlintCliError =
522→        this.let { e ->
523→            when (e) {
524→                is KtLintParseException ->
525→                    KtlintCliError(
526→                        line = e.line,
527→                        col = e.col,
528→                        ruleId = "",
529→                        detail = "Not a valid Kotlin file (${e.message?.lowercase(Locale.getDefault())})",
530→                        status = KOTLIN_PARSE_EXCEPTION,
531→                    )
532→                is KtLintRuleException -> {
533→                    val codeSource = if (code.isStdIn) {
534→                        "code"
535→                    } else {
536→                        "file '${code.fileName}'"
537→                    }
538→                    logger.debug("Internal Error (${e.ruleId}) in $codeSource at position '${e.line}:${e.col}", e)
539→                    KtlintCliError(
540→                        line = e.line,
541→                        col = e.col,
542→                        ruleId = "",
543→                        detail = "Internal Error (rule '${e.ruleId}') in $codeSource at position '${e.line}:${e.col}. " +
544→                            "Please create a ticket at https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues " +
545→                            "and provide the source code that triggered an error.\n" +
546→                            e.stackTraceToString(),
547→                        status = KTLINT_RULE_ENGINE_EXCEPTION,
548→                    )
549→                }
550→                else -> throw e
551→            }
552→        }
553→
554→    private fun readPatternsFromStdin(delimiter: String): Set<String> {
555→        require(delimiter.isNotEmpty())
556→
557→        return String(System.`in`.readBytes())
558→            .split(delimiter)
559→            .let { patterns: List<String> ->
560→                patterns.filterTo(LinkedHashSet(patterns.size), String::isNotEmpty)
561→            }
562→    }
563→
564→    /**
565→     * Executes "Callable"s in parallel (lazily).
566→     * The results are gathered one-by-one (by `cb(<callable result>)`) in the order of corresponding "Callable"s
567→     * in the "Sequence" (think `seq.toList().map { executorService.submit(it) }.forEach { cb(it.get()) }` but without
568→     * buffering an entire sequence).
569→     *
570→     * Once kotlinx-coroutines are out of "experimental" stage everything below can be replaced with
571→     * ```
572→     * suspend fun <T> Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel(...) {
573→     *     val ctx = newFixedThreadPoolContext(numberOfThreads, "Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel")
574→     *     ctx.use {
575→     *         val channel = produce(ctx, numberOfThreads) {
576→     *             for (task in this@parallel) {
577→     *                 send(async(ctx) { task.call() })
578→     *             }
579→     *         }
580→     *         for (res in channel) {
581→     *             cb(res.await())
582→     *         }
583→     *     }
584→     * }
585→     * ```
586→     */
587→    private fun <T> Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel(
588→        cb: (T) -> Unit,
589→        numberOfThreads: Int = Runtime.getRuntime().availableProcessors(),
590→    ) {
591→        val pill = object : Future<T> {
592→            override fun isDone(): Boolean {
593→                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
594→            }
595→
596→            override fun get(
597→                timeout: Long,
598→                unit: TimeUnit,
599→            ): T {
600→                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
601→            }
602→
603→            override fun get(): T {
604→                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
605→            }
606→
607→            override fun cancel(mayInterruptIfRunning: Boolean): Boolean {
608→                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
609→            }
610→
611→            override fun isCancelled(): Boolean {
612→                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
613→            }
614→        }
615→        val q = ArrayBlockingQueue<Future<T>>(numberOfThreads)
616→        val producer = thread(start = true) {
617→            val executorService = Executors.newCachedThreadPool()
618→            try {
619→                for (task in this) {
620→                    q.put(executorService.submit(task))
621→                }
622→                q.put(pill)
623→            } catch (e: InterruptedException) {
624→                // we've been asked to stop consuming sequence
625→            } finally {
626→                executorService.shutdown()
627→            }
628→        }
629→        try {
630→            while (true) {
631→                val result = q.take()
632→                if (result != pill) cb(result.get()) else break
633→            }
634→        } finally {
635→            producer.interrupt() // in case q.take()/result.get() throws
636→            producer.join()
637→        }
638→    }
639→}
640→
641→private class CodeStyleValueConverter : CommandLine.ITypeConverter<CodeStyleValue> {
642→    @Throws(Exception::class)
643→    override fun convert(value: String?): CodeStyleValue =
644→        when (value?.lowercase()?.replace("-", "_")) {
645→            null -> CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
646→            "ktlint_official" -> CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official
647→            "android_studio" -> CodeStyleValue.android_studio
648→            "intellij_idea" -> CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea
649→            else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid code style value")
650→        }
651→}
652→
653→private class LogLevelConverter : CommandLine.ITypeConverter<Level> {
654→    @Throws(Exception::class)
655→    override fun convert(value: String?): Level =
656→        when (value?.uppercase()) {
657→            "TRACE" -> Level.TRACE
658→            "DEBUG" -> Level.DEBUG
659→            "INFO" -> Level.INFO
660→            "WARN" -> Level.WARN
661→            "ERROR" -> Level.ERROR
662→            "NONE" -> Level.OFF
663→            else -> throw IllegalStateException("Invalid log level '$value'")
664→        }
665→}
666→
667→// Do not convert to "val" as the function depends on PicoCli options which are not fully instantiated until the "run" method is started
668→internal fun List<String>.toFilesURIList() =
669→    map {
670→        val jarFile = File(it.expandTildeToFullPath())
671→        if (!jarFile.exists()) {
672→            logger.error { "File '$it' does not exist" }
673→            exitKtLintProcess(1)
674→        }
675→        jarFile.toURI().toURL()
676→    }
677→
678→/**
679→ * Gets the relative route of the path. Also adjusts the slashes for uniformity between file systems.
680→ */
681→internal val Path.relativeRoute: String
682→    get() {
683→        val rootPath = Paths.get("").toAbsolutePath()
684→        return this
685→            .relativeToOrSelf(rootPath)
686→            .pathString
687→            .replace(File.separatorChar, '/')
688→    }
689→
690→/**
691→ * Wrapper around exitProcess which ensure that a proper log line is written which can be used in unit tests for
692→ * validating the result of the test.
693→ */
694→internal fun exitKtLintProcess(status: Int): Nothing {
695→    logger.debug { "Exit ktlint with exit code: $status" }
696→    exitProcess(status)
697→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintParseException
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine

  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleException
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.RuleExecutionRuleFilter
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.RunAfterRuleFilter
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.ruleRunners
 14→import mu.KotlinLogging
 15→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 16→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.FileASTNode
 17→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiElement
 18→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiErrorElement
 19→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.idea.KotlinLanguage
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtFile
 21→
 22→private val KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER = KotlinPsiFileFactoryProvider()
 23→
 24→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 25→
 26→internal class RuleExecutionContext private constructor(
 27→    val code: Code,
 28→    val rootNode: FileASTNode,
 29→    val ruleRunners: Set<RuleRunner>,
 30→    val editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 31→    val positionInTextLocator: (offset: Int) -> LineAndColumn,
 32→) {
 33→    private lateinit var suppressionLocator: SuppressionLocator
 34→
 35→    init {
 36→        rebuildSuppressionLocator()
 37→    }
 38→
 39→    fun rebuildSuppressionLocator() {
 40→        suppressionLocator = SuppressionLocatorBuilder.buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(rootNode)
 41→    }
 42→
 43→    fun executeRule(
 44→        rule: Rule,
 45→        fqRuleId: String,
 46→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 47→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 48→    ) {
 49→        try {
 50→            rule.startTraversalOfAST()
 51→            rule.beforeFirstNode(
 52→                // The rule get access to an EditConfig which is filtered by the properties which are actually registered as being used by
 53→                // the rule. In this way it can be forced that the rule actually registers the properties that it uses and the field becomes
 54→                // reliable to be used by for example the ".editorconfig" file generator.
 55→                editorConfig.filterBy(rule.usesEditorConfigProperties),
 56→            )
 57→            this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(rootNode, rule, fqRuleId, autoCorrect, emit)
 58→            rule.afterLastNode()
 59→        } catch (e: RuleExecutionException) {
 60→            throw KtLintRuleException(
 61→                e.line,
 62→                e.col,
 63→                e.rule.ruleId.value,
 64→                """
 65→                Rule '${e.rule.ruleId.value}' throws exception in file '${code.fileName}' at position (${e.line}:${e.col})
 66→                   Rule maintainer: ${e.rule.about.maintainer}
 67→                   Issue tracker  : ${e.rule.about.issueTrackerUrl}
 68→                   Repository     : ${e.rule.about.repositoryUrl}
 69→                """.trimIndent(),
 70→                e.cause,
 71→            )
 72→        }
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    private fun executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 76→        node: ASTNode,
 77→        rule: Rule,
 78→        fqRuleId: String,
 79→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 80→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81→    ) {
 82→        /**
 83→         * The [suppressionLocator] can be changed during each visit of node when running [KtLintRuleEngine.format]. So a new handler is to
 84→         * be built before visiting the nodes.
 85→         */
 86→        val suppressHandler = SuppressHandler(suppressionLocator, autoCorrect, emit)
 87→        if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 88→            try {
 89→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 90→                    rule.beforeVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→                }
 92→                if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 93→                    node
 94→                        .getChildren(null)
 95→                        .forEach { childNode ->
 96→                            suppressHandler.handle(childNode, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 97→                                this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 98→                                    childNode,
 99→                                    rule,
100→                                    fqRuleId,
101→                                    autoCorrect,
102→                                    emit,
103→                                )
104→                            }
105→                        }
106→                }
107→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
108→                    rule.afterVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
109→                }
110→            } catch (e: Exception) {
111→                if (autoCorrect) {
112→                    // line/col cannot be reliably mapped as exception might originate from a node not present in the
113→                    // original AST
114→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
115→                        rule,
116→                        0,
117→                        0,
118→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
119→                        e.cause ?: e,
120→                    )
121→                } else {
122→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(node.startOffset)
123→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
124→                        rule,
125→                        line,
126→                        col,
127→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
128→                        e.cause ?: e,
129→                    )
130→                }
131→            }
132→        }
133→    }
134→
135→    companion object {
136→        internal fun createRuleExecutionContext(
137→            ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
138→            code: Code,
139→        ): RuleExecutionContext {
140→            val psiFileFactory = KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER.getKotlinPsiFileFactory(ktLintRuleEngine.isInvokedFromCli)
141→            val normalizedText = normalizeText(code.content)
142→            val positionInTextLocator = buildPositionInTextLocator(normalizedText)
143→
144→            val psiFileName =
145→                code.fileName
146→                    ?: if (code.script) {
147→                        "File.kts"
148→                    } else {
149→                        "File.kt"
150→                    }
151→            val psiFile = psiFileFactory.createFileFromText(
152→                psiFileName,
153→                KotlinLanguage.INSTANCE,
154→                normalizedText,
155→            ) as KtFile
156→
157→            psiFile
158→                .findErrorElement()
159→                ?.let { errorElement ->
160→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(errorElement.textOffset)
161→                    throw KtLintParseException(line, col, errorElement.errorDescription)
162→                }
163→
164→            val rootNode = psiFile.node
165→
166→            val editorConfig =
167→                ktLintRuleEngine
168→                    .editorConfigLoader
169→                    .load(code.filePath)
170→                    .also {
171→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
172→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("disabled_rules", "0.49")
173→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
174→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("ktlint_disabled_rules", "0.49")
175→                    }
176→
177→            val ruleRunners =
178→                ktLintRuleEngine.ruleRunners(
179→                    RuleExecutionRuleFilter(editorConfig),
180→                    RunAfterRuleFilter(),
181→                )
182→
183→            return RuleExecutionContext(
184→                code,
185→                rootNode,
186→                ruleRunners,
187→                editorConfig,
188→                positionInTextLocator,
189→            )
190→        }
191→
192→        private fun normalizeText(text: String): String {
193→            return text
194→                .replace("\r\n", "\n")
195→                .replace("\r", "\n")
196→                .replaceFirst(UTF8_BOM, "")
197→        }
198→
199→        private fun PsiElement.findErrorElement(): PsiErrorElement? {
200→            if (this is PsiErrorElement) {
201→                return this
202→            }
203→            this.children.forEach { child ->
204→                val errorElement = child.findErrorElement()
205→                if (errorElement != null) {
206→                    return errorElement
207→                }
208→            }
209→            return null
210→        }
211→    }
212→}
213→
214→private fun EditorConfig.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete(
215→    propertyName: String,
216→    ktlintVersion: String,
217→) {
218→    if (this.contains(propertyName)) {
219→        LOGGER.warn {
220→            "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' is obsolete and is not used by KtLint starting from version $ktlintVersion. Remove " +
221→                "the property from all '.editorconfig' files."
222→        }
223→    }
224→}
225→
226→private class RuleExecutionException(
227→    val rule: Rule,
228→    val line: Int,
229→    val col: Int,
230→    override val cause: Throwable,
231→) : Throwable(cause)
